events.event_title: Bike from Toronto to Kolbe 15
events.event_description:
Part of the Kolbe 15 Rain or Shine series. A ride that earns its finish line.
​For those who know resilience is not built in a boardroom.

​Before we spend a week indoors, this is how we kick off Tech Week. We meet at Finch Station at 7 am, clip in, and ride out of the city, ending at the Kolbe 15 founder hub.

​A support vehicle rides alongside the whole way with snacks and water. It's there if you need it, but you won't want to use it.

​When you arrive, jump in for a swim, then unwind in the sauna and hot tub. If you've still got energy in those legs, shoot pool, take a canoe out, or paddleboard on the water.

​We eat lunch together, gather around the fire, and let the conversation go where it goes, the kind that happens naturally with people who get why we push ourselves.

​We depart at 5pm, everyone gets a ride back to Toronto in a vehicle. You'll be back in the city by 7pm.

​What's included: snacks and water along the ride, lunch, drinks and refreshments at the property, and full use of the property and its amenities.

​The best way to start Tech Week.

events.event_title: AI Build Lab
events.event_description:
From idea → prototype in 1 day. No more waiting.

Most people don’t lack ideas.They lack the space, structure, and support to actually start.

During Toronto Tech Week, we’re changing that.

 Why This Event Exists:

We’ve seen this pattern across thousands of students, professionals, and founders:

“I’ve been thinking about this idea for months…”

“I want to build something but don’t know where to start…”

“I’m not technical, so I don’t think I can…”

The reality today?

👉 AI has changed the game.👉 The barrier to building is lower than ever.👉 But execution is still the biggest gap.

This event is designed to close that gap.

Not through another panel.Not through theory.

But by getting you to actually build something, in the room.

What This Experience Is:

The AI Build Lab is a 5–6 hour guided sprint + workshop + showcase designed to help you go from:

👉 idea → problem clarity → MVP → live demo

All in one day. You’ll work in curated teams (with both technical + non-technical participants), supported by mentors, and move through a structured build process used by real product teams.

As outlined in the workshop structure, each team is supported by a dedicated mentor and follows a clear build framework focused on defining the user, problem, MVP, and execution path .

👀 Who This Is For:

This is intentionally designed to be inclusive of all skill levels:

Developers & engineers
Product managers, designers, operators
Students & early-career professionals
Newcomers exploring opportunities in tech
Non-technical individuals curious about AI
Aspiring founders & side-project builders

👉 You do NOT need:
a full idea
a technical background
prior experience building

Just curiosity + willingness to try.

What You’ll Actually Do

This isn’t a sit-and-listen event.

You will:
Learn how AI tools can be used to build real products
Understand how to think like a product builder
Work in a team to define a real problem
Scope a realistic MVP (not overbuilt ideas)
Build a working prototype using AI tools
Get feedback from mentors
Share your work with the community

What You’ll Leave With:

By the end of the day, you won’t just have “learned something.” You’ll have:
✅ A real project or prototype
✅ Hands-on experience using AI tools
✅ A portfolio-ready build (huge for job seekers)
✅ New collaborators, mentors, and connections
✅ A repeatable framework to keep building

High-Level Agenda

🟢 11:00 – 11:30 | Arrival + Networking

🟢 11:30 – 12:00 | Welcome + Introduction

🟢 12:00 – 1:30 | Skill-Leveling + Live Demo

🟢 1:30 – 2:00 | Team Formation + Scoping

🟢 2:00 – 5:30 | 🚀 Build Sprint (With real-time mentor support throughout)

🟢 5:30 – 6:00 | Closing + Networking

events.event_title: From Idea to Clickable Prototype in Minutes: A Practical Miro AI Workshop
events.event_description: Most teams do not struggle because they lack talent.They struggle because they start building before they are aligned.Ideas move into development too quickly, feedback arrives too late, and teams discover halfway through delivery that everyone had a slightly different vision of the outcome. The result is rework, missed deadlines, frustrated stakeholders, and wasted budget.This hands-on workshop during Toronto Tech Week shows how teams can solve that problem using Miro’s AI-powered prototyping capabilities.Hosted by Generation Digital, a workplace AI consultancy working with organisations across the UK and Canada, this practical session demonstrates how teams can move from a rough concept to a working clickable prototype in minutes.Rather than discussing theory, participants will see the process live and learn how AI-powered prototyping can help teams align earlier in the product, service, and workflow design process.During the session we will explore:• How AI-powered prototyping helps teams visualise ideas faster• How early prototypes reduce costly development rework• How product, marketing, and operations teams can collaborate more effectively around a shared visual concept• How Miro’s AI capabilities accelerate innovation while improving decision makingYou will see how quickly an idea can become something tangible that teams can test, critique, and refine before development begins.The session is designed for leaders and teams responsible for product development, digital transformation, innovation, and operational improvement.If your organisation is looking for faster alignment, clearer communication, and more confident decision making before projects begin, this workshop will show how modern teams are using AI to move from idea to execution far more efficiently.Seats are limited to keep the session practical and interactive.Join us at Toronto Tech Week and see how quickly your next idea could become a working prototype.

events.event_title: TMLS Agentic Hackathon
events.event_description:
Presented by FGF Brands

For the past few years, "building an app" meant months of work, a full team, and a lot of luck.

That's changed.

Today, one person with the right AI tools can go from idea to working product in days. Cursor writes the code. Claude handles the logic. Bolt scaffolds the UI. The bottleneck isn't skill anymore — it's imagination and execution speed.

The TMLS Agentic Hackathon is built around that reality. Five days. Real tools. Ship something that works.

No prescribed problem. No required stack. We want to see what you build when the only constraint is: make it real by Friday.

What does "agentic" mean?

It means your AI isn't just autocompleting lines — it's reasoning, taking actions, and building alongside you. Think less "chatbot," more "co-founder that never sleeps." Cursor, Claude Code, Bolt, Lovable, v0, Replit — use whatever gets you to shipped fastest.

Who should come?

Developers and ML engineers who want to actually ship something. Product people who have an idea and want to build it. First-timers who've been watching from the sidelines and are ready to jump in.

You don't need a team. Show up Monday and we'll find you one.

The week:

📅 Mon May 25 — Kickoff · CSI Spadina, 9 AM Pitches, team formation, project lock. Lunch provided.

💻 Tue–Thu — Build · Wherever you work best Async. Mentor support on Slack.

📬 Thu May 29 — Submit · 2 PM deadline

🏆 Fri May 30 — Expo + Awards · Wisedocs, 12–4 PM Science-fair format. Judges rotate. Winners announced. Lunch + drinks.

How projects are judged:
A panel of 5–7 judges from the TMLS community scores every project on five criteria:
Impact & usefulness — does it solve a real problem for a real person?
Technical execution — did you actually build it, or just mock it up?
Demo quality — can you show it working clearly and confidently?
Novelty & creativity — is there a fresh angle or clever approach?
Shipping mindset — does it feel like something that could go further?

Prizes: 

🥇 Winner — tickets to TMLS 2026 (June 17–18, CIBC Square) 

🥈 Runner-up — sponsor prize kit 

🗳️ People's Choice — voted live by attendees

events.event_title: Market Mindfulness and Finliti Game of Gains Official Launch
events.event_description:
What really drives the stock market — data, or human psychology? Market Mindfulness: The Finliti Game of Gains Fintech Tournament is an interactive experience where participants step into the role of investors and learn how emotions like fear, confidence, regret, and herd behavior shape financial decisions. 

It's a fun, beginner-friendly board game experience inspired by the stock market

After a short tutorial on behavioral finance, players compete in a live strategy tournament inspired by poker, blackjack, and Dungeons & Dragons. Through gameplay, participants navigate bull markets, downturns, commodity shocks, and behavioral traps that mirror real investing scenarios.

Hosted by fintech founder Jennifer Schell (Finliti) and Highline Beta, this event blends financial literacy, strategy, and friendly competition. Broker-dealers and fintech professionals will join the tables, and prizes will be awarded to the top players.

Whether you're a founder, builder, student, or curious about the markets, this is a chance to experience investing in a way that’s social, strategic, and unforgettable.

events.event_title: Boot Sequence: Contrast Therapy, Breathwork & Sauna
events.event_description:
Most founders show up to Tech Week running on yesterday's cache — calendar bloat, half-finished decks, last week's stress still in the system. Boot Sequence is a 90-minute group reset to start the week sharp.

​Guided contrast therapy and breathwork in a small group of founders, builders, and operators. The science is real: heat-cold cycling drops cortisol, regulates the nervous system, and pulls you out of fight-or-flight before the keynotes even start. Breathwork between rounds anchors it.

​You'll leave with a clear head, a regulated nervous system, and a handful of new connections from people who started Tech Week the same way you did.


​What to expect

​Group contrast therapy (large-format sauna + cold plunge tubs) guided by a world-class professional in breathwork

​Breathwork between rounds to anchor the reset

​Functional mocktails afterward — Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, vitamins. Fuel, not depletion.

​Bring swimwear. Towels provided. Phones go in lockers.

​About the space

​NRG Haus is Toronto's first social wellness club — a 5,000 sq ft Liberty Village space combining guided contrast therapy, functional beverages, IV recovery, and intentional social design under one roof. Inside: a 55-person sauna, four immersive cold-plunge tubs each paired with a 16-foot LED screen running ocean waves and icefields, a tea lounge with a water-vapour fireplace, and a zero-proof bar pouring adaptogenic drinks built around Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, and vitamins instead of alcohol. Phones go in lockers. Be fully present. You leave grounded, energized, and clear-headed — not depleted.

events.event_title: Legal Tech Show & Tell
events.event_description:
While the legal industry is often labeled as "tech-averse," a new wave of lawyers is proving the opposite. Join us for a high-density, peer-to-peer "Show & Tell" designed specifically for legal practitioners who have something to share and are looking to learn.

This is a tactical session where we go "under the hood" of our practices. We invite attendees to share one specific tool, automation, or workflow that has fundamentally changed how they practice law—whether it’s AI-driven drafting, custom-built apps, or creative practice management hacks.

Share: Give a 3-minute "lightning demo" of your favorite tech stack element, system or tool.
Learn: Discover what other forward-thinking GTA lawyers are using in their practices.

Who should attend?
Whether you are a solo practitioner or lawyer working in a firm, if you’re obsessed with efficiency and innovation, this room is for you.

Note: This is an intimate session capped at 15–18 people. Light refreshments will be provided. There are a variety of restaurants in walking distance should attendees want to go for lunch together afterwards.

events.event_title: Toronto takes on the world: Winning strategies for global growth
events.event_description:
Going global sounds exciting, until you are faced with choosing the right markets, managing risk, and getting it right the first time.

Toronto Takes on the World brings together founders who have done exactly that. In this candid, in-person session, you will hear how Toronto-based ventures approached international expansion, what worked, what did not, and what they would do differently.

From leveraging the city’s diverse talent and globally connected ecosystem to identifying the right first markets, this conversation focuses on the real decisions behind scaling beyond Canada. You will also gain perspective on today’s shifting economic and political landscape, and learn how Export Development Canada and the Trade Commissioner Service can support your growth.

Join us after the discussion to connect with founders, experts, and peers building globally from Toronto.

events.event_title: Why General AI Breaks in Real Business Workflows
events.event_description: Tried a few AI tools, but they still do not feel right for your business? This session will explore why.General-purpose AI tools can be impressive in demos and helpful for simple tasks, but real business workflows are a different story. Once you add industry rules, live data, customer expectations, and revenue pressure, generic tools can start to break down.In this session, Bryan Xu, Co-founder of DealerAI, will share practical lessons from building and deploying specialized AI in customer-facing workflows, using automotive retail as a case study. We’ll walk through common pitfalls teams run into with general AI, what good looks like in real operating environments, and how to think about integration, quality control, and measurable ROI.You will learn:- Where general AI tends to struggle in real workflows- What makes specialized AI more effective in complex industries- How teams approach integration, quality, and ROI for customer-facing use casesThis session is designed for business owners, operators, and GTM leaders who want real-world AI takeaways. You do not need a technical background, come as you are, and bring questions.Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 26–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com.

events.event_title: PMs in the Age of AI: From Break-In to Breakthrough
events.event_description:
Are you looking to break into, transition within, or thrive in Product Management in the age of AI? This event is for you.

Hosted by Iris Guo, a former AI PM at Zynga, Scale AI, Microsoft, and Google X, and a Career Coach for 3+ years, and Aleeza Ladhani, a PM at Sago Mini, a Harvard M.Ed., and a former chocolate company founder — generously sponsored by Sago Mini — this event blends learning with meaningful connection.

We’ll begin with a speaker panel exploring how AI is reshaping the PM landscape — from navigating career transitions into product to helping mid–senior PMs evolve their skillsets and stay competitive in an AI-first world.

Following the panel, we’ll move into a job search clinic hosted by Iris to offer practical guidance on positioning yourself for your next move — whether you're breaking in, pivoting, or levelling up.

The evening concludes with a happy hour networking session, where you can connect with peers across experience levels — from aspiring PMs to seasoned operators — in a relaxed, community-driven environment.

🗓 Agenda

5:00 – 5:30 PM | Arrival & Check-in
5:30 – 6:30 PM | Speaker Panel
6:30 – 7:30 PM | Job Search Clinic
6:30 – 8:30 PM | Networking & Happy Hour

Whether you're making a career transition, actively job searching, or looking to future-proof your PM skillset, this event is designed to give you the insights and connections to move forward with confidence in the AI era.

events.event_description:
​Most content gets ignored — but raw stories, strong perspectives, and real signal helps founders and startups break through. Join a candid panel on founder-led marketing, plus sessions to record your own story. ​The Founder Signal is a free event hosted by Clarity Content as part of Toronto Tech Week, where we'll discuss what actually makes founder-led marketing work now — not more content for content’s sake, but sharper stories, stronger perspective, and a clearer signal in a noisy market.

​Join us for a panel discussion on founder stories, content, and growth.

​Founders will have access to free content sessions to record a short story or insight. Clarity Content will edit and publish the final videos as part of the experience. (Select time slots will also be opened for founder interview recordings, booked in advance (link sent out to those who register). 

​Schedule:
​3 pm — 5:30 pm | Content Sessions (Pre-Booked)
​5 pm — 5:45 pm | Networking
​6 pm — 7 pm | Panel Discussion and Q&A

​Perfect for founders, operators, and leaders who are tired of bland brand content, tired of sounding like everyone else, and ready to show the thinking behind what they’re building.

​Those breaking through right now are not just louder, they are clearer, more human and more believable. They turn founder insight into trust, attention, and momentum. ​Less posturing, less noise, and more signal. Content that compounds.

​Clarity Content turns founder expertise into high-leverage content assets, helping founders and leaders to build trust, grow their presence, and attract opportunities.

events.event_title: Bet on Yourself: Women in Tech Poker Night
events.event_description:
Poker is more than a game. It’s a masterclass in decision-making, risk, and reading the room.

Bet on Yourself is an intimate, hands-on experience designed for women in tech who want to build confidence in high-stakes situations, both at the table and in their careers.

In a space that has historically been male-dominated, this night is about learning how to take your seat, trust your instincts, and make bold, calculated moves.

No poker experience is required.

We’ll start by teaching the fundamentals of the game, then transition into live play where you’ll practice:

Making decisions with incomplete information
Assessing risk and reward in real time
Managing emotions under pressure
Reading people, not just the cards

This isn’t about becoming a professional poker player.
It’s about building the mindset and skills to navigate negotiations, career moves, and leadership moments with clarity and confidence.

You won’t just learn how to play the game.  You’ll learn how to play your hand.

Seats are intentionally limited to keep the experience personal, interactive, and high-impact.

​The experience takes place in The Portal: a one-of-a-kind somatic sound studio with therapeutic light. Through guided breath patterns, layered sound, and intentional listening, you’ll be invited to shift attention inward—experiencing sound not just through the ears, but through the body.

​The Portal was created as an intimate space for experiential classes, storytelling, and community-led sessions—designed to deepen awareness, regulate the nervous system, and expand how we perceive and process sound.

events.event_title: Why Brand Authority Is Your Startup’s AI Search Strategy
events.event_description: Your brand isn’t your logo anymore - it’s what AI says about you (or doesn't) when making recommendations. When buyers ask ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini for the “best tools” in your category, only a few companies appear. Those companies have built brand authority signals long before buyers ever visit their website. This session shows founders how to design those signals intentionally so AI, media, analysts, and customers recognize their company as a trusted leader.Takeaways for startups:• The difference between branding and brand authority• How AI search decides which companies get recommended• Five authority signals every startup should build early• Practical ways to become the company AI recommends.

events.event_title: Building AI for Enterprise with Instalily x Google
events.event_description:
What does it actually take to deploy AI inside enterprise systems?

InstaLILY builds AI agents that automate workflows across industrial distribution, healthcare, and construction. We're backed by Insight Partners and supported by the Google, NVIDIA, and Microsoft ecosystems.

Join us for a rooftop evening in Toronto with drinks and a fireside conversation between our CEO Amit Shah and Naveen Nigam, who leads Google's Americas Startup and Developer Ecosystem. They'll dig into the InstaLILY journey: how we got here, what works, what breaks, and what changes when AI runs in production. events.event_title: Scale Stage Founder Dinner
events.event_description:
You've built something real. Now let's talk about what comes next, with founders who get it. Past the dreamer phase, past the point solution stage, and still owning the majority of what you've built. You shouldn't have to figure it out alone. Come find your people.

This dinner is intentionally small and curated for founders running scale-stage businesses at $15M+ ARR. There's no pitch at the end of this evening and no hidden agenda beyond good conversation over a meal. Just a room of operators who have grown past the hard early stages and are now navigating the messier, less-documented ones. 

We're Mark and Catherine Graham, and since 2011 we've been growing commonsku from the inside out. We knew the promotional products industry firsthand, saw the need for a workflow platform designed specifically for it, and built it. Fifteen years later, we're proud to serve 900+ distributors and 100+ suppliers powering $2B in GMV. We know what this stage feels like, and how rare it is to find a room of founders who’ve lived it. 

Honestly, this dinner is the one we wish had existed for us at this stage, and Toronto Tech Week felt like the right moment to bring this group together. Expect candid conversations on the things keeping you up at night: growing fast without losing ownership, going deep in a vertical versus broadening your market, finding your footing as a non-technical founder, and figuring out where AI fits into everything you're building. Our hope is that you'll leave with a small circle of founders who become your sounding board long after dinner ends, and at least one idea that changes how you think about what's next.

The Evening
6:30-7:30pm: Beverages and mingling
7:30-9:30pm: Dinner in a Jeffersonian style, where one conversation keeps the whole table connected throughout the evening

If you're compounding past $15M ARR, monetizing GMV, extending your product, or trying to grow without giving away the cap table, this dinner was designed for you.

Seats are limited. Apply to attend. 

About commonsku 
commonsku is a workflow collaboration platform designed exclusively for the promotional products industry. At the core, commonsku is a systems of record platform for promotional products distributors that enables them to manage their customer interactions, present ideas to their customers, create orders, manage production, invoice, and bill. With commonsku’s connected workflow, distributors find joy and growth in their businesses through powerful technology and unmatched community. commonsku.com

About Toronto Tech Week
Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com

events.event_description:

Building a startup is stressful.
Fundraising is weird.
And let’s be honest — half of it feels like a joke anyway.

So we made it one.

But it’s not just a show.

During the standup, we’re opening the room for high-quality networking. No awkward small talk. No random business cards. Fun engagement and real conversations with people who are actually building things.

events.event_title: Cut & Connect: Black Men in Tech
events.event_description:
A curated space dedicated to Black men in tech, aiming to connect with one another in an authentically Black male space: The Barberhood, to exist as we are and discuss topics important to us. An organic, unstructured mixer focused on community and authentic connection during Toronto Tech Week.

The tech industry moves fast, but real connection takes time.

Join us for Cut & Connect, an evening dedicated to Black men navigating the Toronto tech ecosystem. We're stepping out of the office and into The Barberhood - a space that has proven to be a pillar of community and authentic conversation - to connect, share experiences, and exist as we are.

This isn't your typical tech mixer. There are no rigid panels, no formal slide decks, and no forced networking. Instead, we’re hosting an organic, unstructured evening focused on:

Authentic Community: Meet other founders, builders, and professionals who share your journey.

Real Talk: A safe space to discuss the topics that matter most to us in a setting that feels like home.

Good Vibes: Light refreshments, music, and the best atmosphere in the city.

Whether you’re a seasoned executive, a first-time founder, or just starting your career in tech, come through to anchor your Toronto Tech Week in community.

The Details

Date: Monday, May 25, 2026

Time: 7:00 PM – 10:00 PM

Location: The Barberhood, 68 Abell Street, Unit 8, Toronto.

Capacity: Limited to 30 guests to keep the conversation intimate.

Investment: $20

From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com.Please note: By registering for this event, you agree to the Toronto Tech Week Community Guidelines. We require the following information for aggregate reporting purposes only: Title, Company, Industry, City, and Country. Your individual data remains private and confidential with the event hosts.

events.event_title: Float + Reset, Sunrise Paddleboard
events.event_description:
​Part of the Kolbe 15 Rain or Shine series.
​The calm before the tech storm. Start your morning on the water as the sun rises, take a moment to be present, set the mood for the day.

​We meet at Cherry Beach at 6am, get on the water, and paddle out as the sun comes up. No agenda, no pitch, no performance. Just you, the water, and the people around you.

​Once we're out, we stop. For 20 minutes we close our eyes, put the week away, and just be present with the sound of the water, the air, the moment. No experience required. No instruction. Just space to breathe.

​Then we have tea and coffee on the water, a small snack, and talk. The kind of conversation that happens when you're floating on a lake at sunrise with people who get it.

​We paddle back by 8am. You're at your desk by 9am. But you'll feel different for the rest of the day.

events.event_title: The Swedish Way: Capitalising on Culture and Self-Leadership
events.event_description:
Over 25+ years and 5,000 clients across virtually every industry in Europe, we have learnt something that reshaped how we think about building technology: the teams that deliver the best products are not the ones with the best tools or the most rigid processes. They are the ones where people genuinely help one another succeed.

As a Swedish tech consultancy, now in Toronto, we are eager to share our thinking for Tech Week, not just what we do, but how we build products, organise teams without hierarchy and drive breakthroughs at the intersection of technology and culture.

At Netlight, we work without bosses, driven by mentorship, self-leadership and real collaboration. When our consultants join client engineering teams to design, build, and deliver technology, these principles spread: communication improves, ownership grows and teams become truly unified. Culture becomes not just words, but a genuine competitive advantage.

This event is for CTOs, engineering managers and senior tech leaders who recognise that their biggest delivery challenge isn’t always technical. Discover a different perspective, ask tough questions and connect with leaders who are thinking about the same things.

About Netlight
Netlight is an international digital consultancy, co-creating the future today since 1999. With the collective intelligence of 2,200 consultants, we help organisations transform ideas into reality across industries and borders.

events.event_title: Agentic Payments Breakfast - Qatom Launch
events.event_description: The payment layer for the Agentic stack has been missing. Qatom ships it. Join TODAQ Micro for the official launch breakfast — live demo, real Agentic transactions, curated networking. MCP builders, agent founders, and investors active in the Agentic payments infrastructure space. Presented by Hassan Khan, CEO.​ Agentic Payments Breakfast Qatom Launch​ Your agent calls a premium API. The API returns a 402. Your agent doesn't have a wallet, can't sign a transaction, can't negotiate payment terms. So it fails, logs an error, and you handle it manually. That defeats the point of building an autonomous agent.​ This is the problem Qatom was built to solve.​ Qatom is the hosted MCP server that turns any MCP-compatible agent into a fully economic participant. One install. Your agent gets a wallet, discovers paywalled APIs in a live marketplace, and pays for what it calls in the same request. Zero fees. Settled in USD, in under a second. No blockchain, no card rail, no 30-cent floor on a 3-cent transaction.​ TODAQ Micro has been running this infrastructure in production since 2023. This breakfast is the official public launch.​ The gathering is small and curated. Attendees include MCP builders, agent founders, and investors active in the Agentic stack.   We're bringing together the people who are shipping Agentic systems and thinking seriously about what autonomous payments actually requires at the infrastructure level.​ Expect a live demo with agents calling, paying, and earning in real time. A conversation with Hassan Khan, CEO of TODAQ Micro, on what three years of production agent payment infrastructure actually looks like. And an honest discussion on the one layer of the Agentic stack where the existing solutions impose real structural limits.​ The official MCP Dev Summit comes to Toronto in October. This is May. If you want to be in the room when the payment layer ships, this is the morning.​ 80% of enterprise applications shipping today embed at least one AI agent. MCP has crossed 9,400 public servers. The payment layer is the gap. If you're building on MCP, shipping autonomous workflows, or thinking seriously about how agents pay for things, this is your room.​ Registered attendees get hands-on access to TODAQ Console and Twin provisioning, with the team present to onboard you directly.​ Space is intentionally limited.​ Install Qatom: clawhub install Qatom · qatom.ai Open source: github.com/todaqmicro/openclaw

events.event_description:
This ‘Anchored In Innovation Executive Session’ is designed for senior technology leaders responsible for enabling AI-driven transformation while maintaining operational stability, trust, and control. As organizations move beyond experimentation into AI systems that can plan, decide, and act, technology executives sit at the center of a rapidly expanding mandate—balancing innovation velocity with security, governance, and enterprise risk. Anchoram Canada works with organizations operating in regulated, security sensitive, and mission critical environments, specializing in:

•	Data architecture and digital strategy

Harpreet (Harry) Cheema brings hands on experience advising technology leaders where failure is not an option, translating governance and risk into architectural and operational decisions that teams can actually implement.

events.event_title: Two Steps Forward: Career Momentum Lab
events.event_description:
This focused co-working session brings together Mid-career Women in Business and Technology who are planning their next move. Make real progress on your job search, pivot, or consulting path and connect with ambitious peers who inspire momentum and accountability.

---

Join a focused co-working session for Mid-career Women in Business & Technology Professionals who are actively working on their next big career move. This 3-hour experience is designed for those re-branding for a more senior role, navigating a complex job search, making a career pivot, transitioning into consulting or entrepreneurship, or pursuing other efforts to elevate their careers. We'll spend dedicated time in quiet, structured work periods, alternating with intentional networking breaks to connect with like-minded peers who understand the journey. 
Come ready to inspire and be inspired while making meaningful progress alongside driven peers.

SCHEDULE

9:30 am - 9:45 am
Introduce yourself, share what you’re working on, and help us set up the room

9:45 am - 10:30 am
45 mins of quiet work time

10:30 am - 10:45 am
Use this break to connect with your peers

10:45 am - 11:30 am
45 mins of quiet work time

11:30 am - 11:45 am
Use this break to further connect with more peers

11:45 am - 12:15 pm
30 mins of quiet work time

12:15 am - 12:30 pm
Help us tidy up the room

Bonus
Come join us for a tea or coffee after to keep the conversations going at Starbucks on Spadina Ave & Bremner Blvd.

LOCATION
The Fort York meeting room is located on 1st floor of the Fort York library. Wheelchair accessible washrooms are located on 1st and 2nd floors. The closest intersection is Bathurst St & Fort York Blvd. The location is accessible by streetcar. There is paid parking in the area. 

IMPORTANT NOTES
* Please plan to arrive at the library by 9:20 am.
* ​A valid photo ID will be required at event check-in.
* By attending this event, you acknowledge that photography, audio, and video recording may take place.
* This is not a Toronto Public Library event.

ABOUT
Hi, I'm Lana! I help Mid-career Professionals in Business and Technology start having a great time at work again. In my programs, I combine coaching with project management because big career steps require all the help one can get. twostepsforwardconsulting.com

events.event_title: AI Fundamentals: How Executives Are Cutting Through the Noise
events.event_description:
The best leaders right now aren't the ones who know the most about AI. They're the ones who know the right questions to ask and how to act on the answers.  

AI is already on your board's agenda, and you’re registered for this event, you're already thinking about it the right way. This session is about sharpening that thinking. The shift to SaaS didn't require executives to understand the code, it required them to know when and how to move. AI is the same call. 

We'll cut through the noise on what AI actually is. The difference between machine learning, predictive AI, and generative AI isn't just technical; it changes how you evaluate investments, manage risk, and ask the right questions of your team. We'll also look honestly at where GenAI is creating friction inside organizations today and what smart leaders are doing about it. 

From there, we'll get into the frameworks that make AI decisions easier: risk tiers, data governance, and a checklist you can bring back to your leadership team. 

We close with a panel featuring a CFO, an advisory expert, and a technologist sharing what they're seeing on the ground. You’ll get a real perspective from people navigating the same decisions you are. 

You'll leave with: 
- A confident understanding of how AI fits the technology shifts you've already led through 
- Clarity on the types of AI and how to use that knowledge in boardroom conversations 
- Practical insight on where GenAI friction shows up and how to get ahead of it 
- A risk and governance framework that makes AI decisions easier to own

​Most importantly, you’ll leave knowing what not to do. In a space moving this fast, the advantage isn’t in reacting; it’s in making the right calls while others are still figuring it out. This session will help you lead that conversation internally with more clarity and confidence.

events.event_title: Agentic Commerce Pioneers — Demo Day: AI Agents That Pay
events.event_description:
Watch 15 hand-picked teams demo AI agents that book, buy, pay, and settle on their own. Built in 10 days on real payment rails, pitched live to judges from fintech, AI, and payments. $3,000 in prizes across three tracks — autonomous shopping ("The Last Mile"), machine-to-machine micropayments ("Get Paid by Machines"), and agent trust and spend controls ("Agents You Can Trust").

Every AI assistant today gets 90% of the way there and quits. Our teams built the last 10%: agents that don't just recommend — they transact. After each pitch, judges score across four dimensions (functionality, technical depth, real-world potential, presentation) and the audience votes for a People's Choice award.

This is the sharpest two hours of your Toronto Tech Week. Whether you're an engineer, founder, operator, or investor curious about where AI meets money, come see what agentic commerce actually looks like when it ships. Drinks, networking, and sponsor hangouts after the final pitch. Presented by Agnic.AI, as part of the Builder Empowering Program.

events.event_title: Elevate your career – Leveraging the specialist pipeline
events.event_description:
***Back by popular demand***

Advancing your career as a specialist is not a matter of getting that next certification or achieving a higher-level expertise in a particular field, there is more to it than that.

What does your career path look like? What are your options when it comes to developing your career? For many this means becoming a people manager. The question is how do you develop your career *without* transitioning into management? Leveraging the framework of *The Specialist Pipeline*, we will define four dimensions to bring value to organizations that you can hone to elevate your career.

Join us for this interactive workshop where you will self assess and define your path forward to grow your career.
May 25→May 29, 2026 | torontotechweek.com.

events.event_title: The Burnout Invoice: What It's Costing Your Tech Team And the Tools to Fix It
events.event_description:
The tech industry moves fast. Deadlines don't wait, sprints don't slow down, and somewhere between the Slack notifications and the all-hands meetings, your team is running on fumes.
But here's what most companies aren't tracking: burnout isn't just a wellness problem. It's a performance problem, a retention problem, and a leadership problem — and it's showing up on your bottom line whether you're measuring it or not.
The Burnout Invoice is a free, no-fluff virtual webinar designed specifically for tech leaders, HR professionals, and people teams who want to understand what burnout is actually costing their organizations — and walk away with real tools to do something about it.
This isn't a lecture about self-care. This is a practical, honest conversation about what's happening inside your teams, why high-performing people are burning out fastest, and what leaders can do right now to change the trajectory.

What You'll Learn:
In 60 focused minutes, you'll gain:
— A clear, clinical understanding of burnout: what it is, what it isn't, and why stress management advice alone isn't enough
— The hidden organizational costs of burnout including lost productivity, increased absenteeism, and high turnover — with context for what that means in the tech sector specifically
— How to spot burnout early in your team before it becomes a resignation letter or a breakdown
— Practical, evidence-based tools you can implement as a leader or HR professional — without overhauling your entire culture overnight
— A downloadable resource to take back to your team, your manager, or your next planning cycle

Who This Is For:
This webinar is built for people who carry responsibility for others at work:
— Tech managers and team leads who want to support their people without burning out themselves
— HR professionals and people operations teams navigating mental health in high-pressure environments
— Founders and executives who are starting to notice the cracks in their team's performance and culture
— Anyone in the tech industry who suspects burnout is closer than they'd like to admit

Why This Conversation Matters Now:
Burnout in the tech sector is not new — but it is getting worse. The rise of remote work, always-on communication culture, layoffs, and relentless pressure to deliver have created a perfect storm for psychological exhaustion. And yet most organizations are still responding with ping pong tables and pizza Fridays.
The research is clear: burnout is a systemic issue, not a personal failing. It requires a systemic response. This webinar gives you the language, the data, and the tools to start building one.

About Your Host:
This webinar is hosted by Grey Couch Counselling, a practice dedicated to supporting the mental health and psychological wellbeing of individuals navigating high-pressure professional environments. events.event_title: Your Best AI Strategy Has No UI
events.event_description:
The most common AI mistake isn't picking the wrong tool. It's adding another one. 

The executives building real AI advantage aren't expanding their tech stack; they're making their existing stack more intelligent. The future of AI for enterprise is a layer that sits across your existing business systems, connecting your ERP, CRM, finance tools, and HR platforms into something that actually works together. Once that layer exists, something more powerful becomes possible: your people stop clicking through software and start talking to it. 

Natural language is the future of how businesses interact with their systems. Not a chatbot bolted onto one platform, but a single intelligent layer that understands your business, follows your rules, and executes across every system you already run. 

The leaders getting ahead aren't shopping for more tools. They're rethinking how their entire organization interacts with the ones they have. 

You'll leave with: 
- An understanding of AI as a connected layer across your business and not another point tool 
- A clear picture of how natural language is replacing the click-through workflows your teams navigate every day 
- A practical lens for evaluating where this shift creates the most value in your existing stack 
- The vendor questions that separate real capability from a good demo 
- The goal isn't to move fast. It's to move right. You’ll leave this session with a clearer path forward.

Canadian healthcare transformation.Anchoram Consulting Canada is pleased to host an executive roundtable featuring Dr. Thomas Kelly, Co-Founder & CEO of Heidi (Australia) and Dr. Rashaad Bhyat, Senior Clinical Leader at Canada Health Infoway, for a moderated discussion with Anne Fazzalari, Managing Director at Anchoram Canada, on the future of AI in healthcare.Bringing together perspectives from Australia and Canada, this session will explore how leading global practices can be translated into scalable impact within the Canadian healthcare system.Dr. Kelly shares the perspective of a physician turned founder, building AI solutions to reduce administrative burden, address burnout, and restore time for patient care.Dr. Bhyat offers a national view, advancing digital health adoption across Canada and shaping interoperability, clinician engagement, and system-wide innovation.Anne Fazzalari, will highlight how structured change management accelerates adoption by building clinician awareness, trust, and engagement, aligning solutions to real workflows, and reinforcing use through measurable impact.Among our discussion topics, we’ll explore:•How AI is moving from pilot to real-world clinical impact•What it takes to drive clinician adoption at scale•Patient impact of AI•The role of interoperability, data quality, and workflow integration•How global lessons from Australia can be applied in CanadaDesigned for senior clinical, technology and policy leaders, this roundtable focuses on moving beyond experimentation to measurable transformation. events.event_title: Czech Tech Day
events.event_description:
Are you curious about European tech, research, and innovation?
Join us for an afternoon exploring research and innovation cooperation between Canada and the Czech Republic — and discover what's possible when you connect with one of Central Europe's fastest-growing innovation ecosystems.
Organized in collaboration by the Czech Consulate General in Toronto, CzechInvest Agency, and the South Moravian Innovation Centre, this event offers:
Tangible opportunities for research cooperation under Horizon Europe and other funding instruments
Practical insights into the Czech innovation ecosystem
An overview of the thriving Czech Life Sciences and Biotech sector

WHO WILL YOU MEET?
Network over snacks and Czech beer with leading innovation and research institutions, including:
South Moravian Innovation Centre
The City of Brno
South Moravian Region
Prague.bio
Brno University of Technology
Masaryk University
University of Chemistry and Technology

WHO IS THIS FOR?
Professionals interested in Central Europe's innovation landscape, research collaboration opportunities, and growth in Life Sciences and Engineering.

Biotech: The biotech sector in the Czech Republic builds on a century-old industrial tradition, with pharmaceuticals being manufactured in Prague on an industrial scale for over 100 years, and combines that with a cutting-edge scientific inquiry supported by a robust network of over 400 companies and research institutes, a specialized talent pipeline of 12,000 students in life sciences, and a growing venture capital landscape.

What makes the Czech Republic particularly attractive is its concentration of talent and output, with more than 50% of top-quality publications produced in collaboration with partners in the United States, Germany, and the United Kingdom. The country generates a high density of high-impact scientific research relative to its size.

Advanced manufacturing: The Czech Republic is a European leader in advanced manufacturing, combining a long-standing engineering tradition with cutting-edge innovation. Manufacturing contributes to more than 25% of the country’s gross value added, one of the highest shares in Europe, and high-tech products already make up over 20% of Czech exports. The Czech Republic also ranks 8th globally in Harvard’s Economic Complexity Index, underlining the sophistication and diversity of its industrial base. That is supported with a rapid expansion of AI adoption in the Czech Republic, with Czech startups outpacing their European counterparts with AI innovation.

South Moravian Region: The South Moravian Region has emerged as one of the leading European innovation hubs. A global leader in electron microscopy, with one-third of the world's electron microscopes coming from Brno, Brno region excels also in the semiconductor, space, cybersecurity, and game development sectors. Leading the Czech Republic in knowledge intensity and high-tech employment, Brno region matches Europe's top regions. The share of expenditures on science and research relative to regional GDP, at 3.1%, is not only the highest in the Czech Republic over the long term but also reaches the level of knowledge intensity of leading European regions, especially in Germany, Austria, Belgium, or Sweden.

events.event_title: From 1 to Scale: Hiring, Building, and Surviving the Growth Curve
events.event_description:
You figured out how to get from zero to one - but scaling? That’s a different game entirely.

The truth most founders learn the hard way: the hiring instincts that got you here won’t get you there. The wrong hires at this stage don’t just slow you down—they quietly cap your growth, dilute your culture, and cost you real momentum.

This Scale Lab session, powered by Quantumscale, a leading recruitment-on-demand partner, is designed for founders who are serious about building companies that scale with intention. We’re getting into the real strategy behind building a talent roster that can actually carry your next phase of growth, not just keep up with it.

Hear sharp, unfiltered insights from the Quantumscale team, plus real talk from founders and operators who’ve scaled and learned what works (and what doesn’t). 

We’re talking:
- The hires that unlock growth—and the ones that stall it
- When to level up your team (and when you’re too early)
- How to avoid reactive hiring decisions that cost you later
- How to position your company so the right people want in

If you’re building fast, feeling the pressure to “get hiring right,” or starting to realize your current team structure won’t take you where you want to go—this is the room you want to be in.

Come for the strategy. Stay for the real talk.

Drawing on Jonathan’s 20+ years of experience leading large-scale SaaS and engineering teams, the discussion will bridge the gap between technical strategy and industry-wide innovation.

​​Discussion topics will include:
➡️ Building in Canada: The unique opportunities and challenges of the Canadian legal tech ecosystem.
➡️ Successful Scaling: Lessons in product development, user experience and maintaining best-in-class customer retention.

Enjoy an afternoon pick-me-up of coffee, tea, and light snacks while connecting with fellow innovators, professionals and Toronto’s vibrant legal tech community.

​​Whether you are an innovator, legal professional, student, or law tech enthusiast, request your spot today!

​Meet the Speakers:
​Johnathan Watson
Chief Technology Officer, Clio
Jonathan is the Chief Technology Officer at Clio. With over 20 years of leadership experience in AI transformation, large-scale SaaS, and live gaming solutions, Jonathan’s unique approach and passion for intertwining product development, design, user experience, and engineering has accelerated Clio’s product growth while earning Clio best-in-class NPS and customer retention rates. Outside of the office, he’s an endless tinkerer who is always exploring creative pursuits including 3D printing, cooking, and making his daughters laugh.

​Dr. Sasha Saunders
Director, Legal Innovation Zone

She has over a decade of experience building innovation ecosystems in the UK and Canada, including creating, leading and growing a university-wide venture support service from the ground up. As a champion of the intersection of law, technology, and entrepreneurship, Sasha is passionate about empowering innovators to develop the skills and solutions to create meaningful impact. events.event_title: Preparing for CMMC and CPCSC: What Defense Suppliers Need to Know
events.event_description: Canadian and U.S. defense contractors are facing a new compliance reality: CMMC in the United States and CPCSC in Canada. For small and mid-sized suppliers, the challenge is not just understanding the frameworks, but choosing an implementation approach that fits their business, customer base, and budget. This interactive workshop will help Ontario-based defense contractors, subcontractors, and technology suppliers compare CMMC and CPCSC, understand where the frameworks overlap, and explore practical implementation options for smaller teams. Sponsored by CyberCare Pro and PreVeil the session will show how organizations can reduce complexity, protect controlled information, and build a compliance roadmap that supports both Canadian and cross-border opportunities.  Participants will leave with:1. A clear comparison of CMMC and CPCSC requirements.2. A framework for deciding whether to self-assess, outsource, or hybridize compliance work.3. Practical implementation options for identity, device, document, and email protection.4. Guidance on how to align cybersecurity controls with defense contracting goals.5. A better understanding of funding and support options for Ontario-based companies pursuing certification.

events.event_title: Mapping the Pulse of the Grid: Space, Time, and Physics. ⚡🌍
events.event_description:
Mapping the Pulse of the Grid: Space, Time, and Physics. ⚡🌍 

Join Kraftgene AI for a Toronto Tech Week deep-dive into the "Big Three" of Energy AI. See how we’re building the universal intelligence layer for a decentralized future using GNNs, LSTMs/Transformers, and Physics-Informed Learning.

As the global energy transition accelerates, our aging power grids are facing unprecedented stress. Kraftgene AI is at the forefront of this challenge, developing "EnergyEminence"—an AI-native platform designed as a comprehensive framework for orchestrating stability across the entire energy ecosystem.

In this intimate, high-level technical workshop, our founding team will pull back the curtain on:

1. Spatial Intelligence (GNNs): Why Graph Neural Networks are the superior architecture for mapping complex energy networks, from Microgrids to EV charging.

2. Temporal Forecasting (LSTMs/Transformers): Mastering the "Time" element—predicting the exact timing and propagation paths of cascade failures before they occur to enable proactive de-energization.

3. Physics-Informed Learning: Bridging the gap between "Black Box" AI and power engineering to ensure industrial-grade reliability.

4. Interactive Demo: A live, real-time look at our models simulating and mitigating grid risks.

Whether you are a utility professional, an AI researcher, or an investor interested in the "AI for Sustainability" movement, join us to see how we are leveraging the Toronto tech ecosystem to build a more resilient global grid.

Event Details:

📅 Date/Time: May 26, 2026 | 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM EDT

📍 Location: 1370 Don Mills Road, Unit 5, Toronto, ON

🎟️ Tickets: $20 CAD (Includes complimentary coffee/tea and on-site parking)

🔗 Register: https://luma.com/wiupfm5m

(Note: Capacity is limited to 40 guests. Registration is subject to host approval to ensure a high-quality networking environment.)

events.event_title: Krista Kim’s Heart Space at Toronto Tech Week 2026
events.event_description:
Schulich’s Office of Innovation & Entrepreneurship, Pursuits Inc, and Demo Room present a flagship event exploring the intersection of art, artificial intelligence, biometrics, and digital trust.

The first public announcement and advance look inside the Heart Space Canadian exhibition tour (2027), a groundbreaking immersive digital artwork by internationally acclaimed artist Krista Kim, as she returns home following years of international exhibitions in Hong Kong, London, Barcelona, Seoul, Dubai, Las Vegas, New York, and more.

Heart Space is the world’s first collaborative, AI-generative immersive artwork driven by real-time biometric data. Participants’ heartbeats (their unique biometric imprint) are translated into dynamic colours and waveforms, creating a continuously evolving collective composition….

It is also an opportunity to understand the groundbreaking technology of Tenbeo (a Brussels-based biotechnology company pioneering identity authentication through advanced biometric AI) and technology provider behind the exhibition.

As AI advances, protecting digital identities and proof-of-humanity protocols are key to safeguarding human agency, creativity, and dignity… 

Join us to hear Krista Kim and an amazing panel of arts and technology leaders explore these crucial concepts and delve into "proof-of-humanity" in the age of Artificial Intelligence.

What to expect at The Launch Announcement:
- Welcome & national launch announcement
- Keynote by Krista Kim 
- Cross-disciplinary panel discussion
- Immersive art activation in Demo Room (Toronto's hottest new event venue)
- Live biometric demonstrations
- Networking reception and startup tech demos
- Event after-party, featuring DJ 4KORNERS and Chef P

How can our government respond to this moment of rapid change with effective and common-sense methods to safeguard our country’s security? The Samara Centre for Democracy is Canada’s leading non-partisan organization focused on strengthening and protecting Canadian democracy.  Speakers:  Sabreena Delhon - CEO, Samara Centre for Democracy. Beatrice Wayne - Director of Research & Policy, Samara Centre for Democracy. Other speakers to be announced

You’ll walk out with best practices that you can apply wherever you work, or for your own use cases, especially since running AI workloads costs so much on many levels.

events.event_title: From Signal to Execution: The AI Trading Frontier
events.event_description:
Moomoo Trade will host an exclusive, high-impact session at its Bloor Street flagship store, bringing together advanced traders, technologists, and media for a first look at the next evolution of AI-powered investing.
This is not another conversation about AI-generated insights. It’s about what comes next.

From Signal to Execution: The AI Trading Frontier explores the shift from information overload to intelligent action, where trading strategies can be expressed in natural language and executed with institutional-grade precision. As the first platform to bring this capability to market in Canada, moomoo is setting a new standard for how investors interact with markets—transforming intent into execution with unprecedented speed and accessibility.

Attendees will experience how moomoo’s newly launched API Skills are redefining the interface between human intent and market execution, enabling faster, more structured decision-making without the friction of traditional coding or manual workflows.

Through a live demonstration and expert discussion, the session will unpack:
•	How “agentic” AI is transforming trading from reactive to systematic 
•	The closing gap between retail and institutional capabilities 
•	Practical applications of zero-code strategy execution in real market conditions 
•	The evolving role of AI as an assistive layer—enhancing, not replacing, trader control 

This event offers media and sophisticated market participants a front-row seat to a pivotal shift in how trading decisions are made—and executed.

events.event_title: AI Won’t Fix Commerce — But This Might
events.event_description: AI is everywhere - but selling physical products online is still full of friction. ​From unclear product visualization to endless content needs and high return rates, the core experience of buying physical goods hasn’t evolved as fast as the technology around it. This is a small, curated roundtable for founders, operators, and product leaders to explore what’s actually changing—and what’s not.

events.event_title: Who Gets to Live Longer? How AI Is Making Longevity Accessible.
events.event_description: Unlock the future of well-being! Join top longevity experts for a powerful panel discussion on preventative health and wearable tech. Network over a healthy, organic, high-protein dinner. Get actionable insights to master your vitality.​Longevity used to be a niche obsession for the ultra-wealthy. Now, AI is making preventive health care more accessible, personalized, and commercially viable.Join our founders, clinicians, and investors to discover:- ​Why longevity is moving from luxury to mainstream and no longer needs a $2M/year, Bryan Johnson budget​- What building in this space looks like, from engineering a dog vaccine to a DIY cancer cure that actually worked​- Why the first single-entrepreneur, billion-dollar company emerged in this space and the opportunity this presents for founders, clinicians and investors ​- How you can take action on living longer right away with wearables, blood tests and moreSpeakers​- Gilberto Gandra — Founder & Investor, Archangel Axion Fund, giving the consumer perspective on investing in longevity innovation while taking his own preventative health just as seriously.- ​Balaji Gopalan — Digital Health Advisor; Ex-Founder/CEO, MedStack and industry insider who knows what it takes to earn clinical and patient trust.- ​Rex Farrand — Co-founder & CEO of MyHealthspan and accessible tech provider, building longevity tools at a price point that makes preventative health available beyond the executive health crowd.​- Ambuj Srivastava — IT Leader & Cancer Patient Advocate, giving voice to patients navigating the sick care system, seeking the advantages and alternatives offered in the longevity space.

events.event_title: AI at Lyft: From Data Foundations to Shipping Faster
events.event_description:
AI only works when the foundation does. In this fast-paced lightning session, two Lyft practitioners cover both ends of the spectrum—followed by a panel discussion on lessons learned, trade-offs, and what's next.

Part 1 — Unifying Data and Metadata for Reliable AI
Shankar Vedaraman, Head of Corporate Data & Analytics

Most AI failures aren't model failures—they're data failures. Shankar shares how Lyft is converging a fragmented data estate into a metadata-driven platform, the prerequisite for trustworthy AI at enterprise scale.

Part 2 — Expanding Lyft to Europe with Cursor and Claude
Arshiya Sayyed, Engineering

A continent-scale Lyft + FreeNow integration should have taken a year of ramp-up. It didn't. Arshiya's team is building Roaming—letting Lyft riders hail rides across Europe—and she'll share how Cursor powered by Claude transformed how they plan, write, and review code.

We'll close with a panel discussion, connecting data foundations to developer velocity and exploring what it really takes to ship AI-powered systems faster in production. We'll share our perspectives on transforming Lyft's engineering towards AI-first development.

events.event_title: Where AI Meets Hospitality
events.event_description: ​The best service businesses in the world have always competed on how they make people feel. AI is changing what that's possible to deliver, and what it costs to fall short.This event is for operators, founders, and investors who believe hospitality is a competitive advantage, not a department. We'll get into how AI is reshaping the front-line member experience in fitness, wellness, and service businesses, like what's actually working in production, where the gaps still are, and what the best operators are doing differently.The evening includes a fireside chat with a leading Toronto wellness operator on what it really takes to deliver a consistent, high-touch experience at scale, and where AI fits into that picture. Followed by drinks and conversation with a curated room of people building and running hospitality-driven businesses.

events.event_title: Loop Tech Talk 1: Shipping Quality at Speed: How We Built a Safety Net for Fintech Releases
events.event_description:
Join us for our Toronto Tech Week x Loop Tech series!

What we'll cover: How Loop Financial transformed from "move fast and break things" to "move fast with confidence." Covering the engineering culture shift, tooling, and processes that let a small team ship rapidly to production while maintaining SOC2 compliance and avoiding customer-facing incidents.

Key topics:
Trunk-based development with strict branch protection
CI/CD pipeline design: SAST, SCA, container scanning, and approval gates
Just-In-Time production access with Icebreak (15-minute auto-expiring access)
Ownership culture: what it really means to "own quality" of a domain service
Blameless post-mortems and continuous improvement loops

Whether you’re scaling a fintech platform, modernizing your DevOps practices, or trying to reconcile velocity with reliability, this talk offers practical lessons you can apply immediately. Come learn how to move fast—with confidence.

events.event_title: Smart Routes: Engineering Trust in the Data Journey
events.event_description: As data becomes the backbone of decision-making, trust becomes its most critical asset. This session introduces Smart Routes, a modern approach to data engineering that applies supply chain principles to automate, streamline, and build confidence throughout the data journey. Leave with a blue-print for:Automating the Data Journey from Source to Insight. Discover how standardized connectors, automated quality checks, and reusable ingestion pipelines reduce manual effort and improve reliability.Building Confidence with Observability and Governance. Execute telemetry, lineage tracking, and policy-as-code for creating a control tower for your data estate—ensuring every data product is traceable, compliant, and trusted.Focusing on Strategic Impact, Not Operational Overhead. Shift from reactive troubleshooting to proactive optimization, enabling teams to deliver trusted insights faster and with greater efficiency.Engineer trust into your data journey to unlock smarter decisions, reduce technical debt, and create a self-improving data supply chain.

events.event_title: The Outbound Grind: High-Impact Sales in the AI Age
events.event_description:
Outbound is arguably the hardest part of the go-to-market journey.
​Whether you're a founder wearing the sales hat, a first GTM hire, or a growing team trying to find a repeatable motion, the distance between "zero" and "booked meeting" feels massive in an AI-saturated world.
We're stripping away the guesswork and diving into the reality of outbound in the AI age.
​No fluff, no theory.
​Just what actually works to build a pipeline that converts.
​The presentation and workshop will be led by outbound sales expert Paul Griffin, Co-Founder and CEO of The Sales Factory and PitchPilot.ai, who's built the playbook behind thousands of outbound campaigns for B2B companies across North America.
We're not standing at the front of the room reading slides. We're live-scripting a real campaign on the spot and sharing the ICP framework we use to cut through the noise.
​Every attendee gets the toolkit and exclusive access to tech stack to launch their first campaigns the next morning.
Come for the strategy, stay for the networking and food!

events.event_title: AI & Defence Innovation Summit 2026: The Future of Digital Trust & Defence Innovation
events.event_description:
AI & Defence 2026

​AI and Defence 2026: AI Trust and Defence Innovation is a premier 3.0‑hour executive summit taking place in Toronto, Canada, on May 26. This hybrid event brings together leading voices from defence, augmented and artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, aerospace, academia, and government.

​Co‑hosted by James Castle, Chairperson of the Cyber Security Global Alliance, and the CSGA Cyber AI Research Institute, along with Master of Ceremony Samir Azizi, Co‑Partner of Toronto DAO, and Toronto Tech Week, this summit will explore the future of AI trust, AI weaponization, autonomous defence systems, quantum and AI‑ready security, next‑generation defence innovations, and the governance frameworks shaping global security over the decade ahead.

​Designed for senior executives, policymakers, military leaders, defence innovators, advanced technology strategists, and Toronto Tech Week participants, this mini summit delivers a powerful combination of strategic insights, practical defence models, and an immersive crisis simulation that challenges participants to navigate real‑world national security threats.

​Through expert dialogue and focused analysis, attendees will gain a strong understanding of the future of AI trust, secure AI systems, autonomous defence technologies, drone‑based threat response, and quantum‑era cybersecurity.

​This event offers a rare opportunity to examine how trusted AI, cross‑border governance standards, and resilient defence capabilities are shaping North America's strategic advantage. It will help leaders prepare for the next era of rapidly accelerating technological conflict and innovation.

​To see our list of in‑person and virtual speakers or to visit our sponsorship page, please go to the CSGA website and select the Events tab.

​We Are Seeking Event Sponsors

​Cyber Security Global Alliance and Toronto DAO are actively seeking sponsors for this event.

​Hybrid Event Details

​This event is hosted in person in downtown Toronto with an option for virtual attendance for guests outside the city. Several speakers will join via our main event screen and audio system, and all registered participants will have the opportunity to ask questions after each presentation to both in‑person and virtual presenters.

​All attendees must be registered. At the registration table, please be prepared to show your assigned QR code.

​Thank you.

Join us for an evening of live demos, honest project updates, and real conversations about what it looks like to build in the open. No polished keynotes. No pitch competitions. Just founders, makers, and builders sharing what they're actually working on — the breakthroughs and the breakdowns.

events.event_title: The Hidden Tradeoffs of AI: What’s Changing Inside Teams
events.event_description:
Many organizations have figured out how to adopt AI. Fewer have figured out what it costs them.

Across teams moving faster with AI, the same tradeoffs keep surfacing: speed at the expense of depth, independence at the expense of connection, pace at the expense of absorption. Leaders are seeing it. Teams are feeling it. Most just aren't saying it out loud yet.

The good news is that these tradeoffs are navigable but only if you're willing to look at them honestly. The organizations getting this right aren't the ones moving fastest. They're the ones paying closest attention.

This session brings together leaders who are navigating these trade-offs in real time. We'll get specific about what's actually breaking down, what's working, and what it takes to build teams that are both AI-capable and genuinely resilient. 

Panelists will be announced shortly.

events.event_title: MCP Server Builder Series: Ship an AI Integration in 2 Hours: React + Apollo + MCP Apps
events.event_description:
You've built a React app. It has data, logic, and real functionality. Now imagine letting an AI agent actually use it, displaying your UI right there in the chat window for users to interact with!

In this 2-hour hands-on workshop, Apollo GraphQL shows frontend developers how to take an existing web app and wire it up to an AI agent end-to-end. No prior GraphQL experience required! You'll leave with working code and a pattern you can use to embed any app into any MCP Apps-compatible host (like ChatGPT, Claude, and others).

What you'll do:
Explore an existing React + GraphQL application
Expose your app's operations as AI-callable tools
Use MCPJam's widget emulator to test against any LLM in the playground

What you'll learn:
How to think about your existing React app as an AI-ready product with GraphQL & MCP
How Apollo Skills helps you build with confidence

Who this is for:
This is a frontend-first workshop. If you know React and have built or consumed a REST API before, you're ready!

Prerequisites:
Attendees should arrive with:
A laptop with Node.js v18+ and npm
A code editor
(Optional, if you want to add your app to ChatGPT without using the MCPJam emulator) A ChatGPT Plus account with OpenAI Apps SDK access

We'll send you a reminder email prior to the workshop!

Food and drinks will be provided.

events.event_title: The Human Edge — A conversation on AI and the future of work
events.event_description: Presented by Re-Work · Toronto Tech Week 2025 AI is moving faster than most people are ready for.The productivity gains are real. But so are the consequences of that productivity. Since the industrial revolution, we’ve used productivity as a measure of the value and worth of an individual, it’s no wonder that the looming evolutionary threat of AI feels so scary for many modern workers. How do we respond to a future of work where productivity is achieved through agentic AI instead of human effort. As a career and work wellbeing organization, we have thoughts.In this curated and honest we’ll discuss where work is heading and what that means for the people doing it. And the ways we’ll need to shift our thinking around work and productivity.Join us for a keynote and facilitated discussion exploring the intersection of AI, automation, and human well-being. We'll get into the big picture and bring it back to what leaders and individuals can do to navigate the AI revolution in work.

events.event_title: AI & Industry Unplugged: presented by PRAKTIKAI - Toronto Tech Week Networking
events.event_description:
​Join PRAKTIKAI for an exciting evening of conversation, connection, and forward‑thinking exploration during Toronto Tech Week. This networking event brings together leaders, innovators, and decision‑makers from across industries who are looking to integrate AI into their operations.

​We’ll kick off with a short, high‑impact presentation from the PRAKTIKAI team, offering a glimpse into practical AI applications that are transforming workflows today. From there, the night is all about meaningful connections, an opportunity for managers, directors, and executives to exchange ideas, share challenges, and meet like‑minded professionals shaping the future of AI in business.

​Whether you're actively exploring automation or simply curious about what’s possible, this session is open to leaders across sectors, including tech startups and investors, traditional sectors like manufacturing and supply chain, legal and beyond. This event offers a relaxed, engaging environment to spark new conversations and discover new opportunities.

events.event_title: Generative design at scale

events.event_title: Is Your Startup One Announcement Away From Irrelevant?
events.event_description:
OpenAI ships. Anthropic ships. Google ships. And sometimes — they ship your product.

​This event is for anyone building with AI who's asked themselves: is what I'm building defensible, or am I one changelog away from being obsolete?

​We'll give you a framework to answer it honestly — and together.

​What we'll cover:

​The difference between a feature and a company — and how to tell which one you're building

​How to identify your actual moat: is it your data, your workflow, or your trust?

​What "platform risk" really means and how to stress-test your idea against it

​How to build for a world where the floor keeps moving

​What to expect:

​A practical framework you can apply to your own product or idea

​Open discussion with founders, builders, and operators who are wrestling with the same questions

​Networking with the community that's actually building

events.event_title: Claude Cowork for Creators: Building an AI Workspace That Actually Knows You
events.event_description:
​If you've tried Cowork or any AI assistant and walked away thinking "this is fine, I guess," you already know the problem: the tools are powerful but the setup is empty. Every session starts cold, with no memory of who you are, what you make, or how you think.

​Sean Oulashin is Stan's Senior Video Editor a Creator (500K+ across platforms). He spent the last several months building "Thinkspace", a done-for-you Cowork setup specifically for Creators.

​In this 45-minute workshop, Sean will show you:

​- The two foundational files every Cowork workspace needs, and why most setups skip them
- ​The exact interview process he uses to build a creator's identity file in under 20 minutes
- How to wire Cowork into the tools creators already use (Notion, Granola, Slack, calendar, Drive)
- Where Cowork breaks for creators specifically, and the workarounds he's tested
- A live walkthrough of his own Thinkspace, so you can see what "good" looks like

​Who this is for:
​- Creators publishing content who want AI to actually help, not generate slop
- Solopreneurs and operators running their own brand or business

​You don't need to be technical to attend! Sean built Thinkspace specifically for non-engineers.

events.event_title: The Agentic Boardroom: Canada's AI Leadership Conversation

​We will open with a keynote from Aaqib Azeem, Global AI and ML Partner Lead at Google, on where enterprise AI is heading. From there, the conversation moves into practical leadership questions: adoption, governance, workflow ownership, risk, and the operating muscle required to turn AI from scattered experimentation into real company leverage
​
Then the room goes to work.

​Guests will be invited into the conversation through live Q&A, practitioner discussion, and facilitated boardroom roundtables

events.event_title: Brand Health: Building Brands People Feel — Fireside + Sober Party at Canada's First Non-Alcoholic Bar
events.event_description:
A fireside chat with Ivan Ho and Kyle Collier, moderated by Ryan Peerwani — followed by Toronto Tech Week's most unexpected after-party: a professional DJ, full sauna and cold plunge, and Canada's first fully non-alcoholic bar.

​Run of show

​7:00–7:30 — Welcome in the sauna. Get loose, meet the room, settle in.

​7:30–8:00 — Fireside chat with Ivan Ho (NRG Haus) and Kyle Collier (Neobanc), moderated by Ryan Peerwani

​8:00–10:30 — Sober party. Pro DJ, full mocktail bar, sauna and cold plunge open all night.

​The founders

​Ivan Ho is the founder of NRG Haus and the operator behind Fit Factory Fitness — recognised as Toronto's Best Boot Camp, Best Gym, and Best Fitness Club multiple times. Thirteen years of building a fitness brand people are genuinely devoted to, and a new venture (the one you're sitting in) that opened to a waiting community before it ever ran an ad.

​Kyle Collier is the CEO and Co-founder of Neobanc — a Systems Design Engineer from UWaterloo, serial fintech entrepreneur with 15+ years of experience and multiple exits, who has grown past startups to over 80 million users globally and spoken at the United Nations on fintech innovation. Neobanc itself? 800% growth in five months on only half a million dollars of funding — the math only works when users bring users. That's not a funnel. That's a cult.

​Different industries, same thesis: brands people feel aren't built by marketing. They're built by founders who understand what they're actually solving, then earn trust one user at a time. The brand is the relationship.


​About the space

​​NRG Haus is Toronto's first social wellness club — a 5,000 sq ft Liberty Village space combining guided contrast therapy, functional beverages, IV recovery, and intentional social design under one roof. Inside: a 55-person sauna, four immersive cold-plunge tubs each paired with a 16-foot LED screen running ocean waves and icefields, a tea lounge with a water-vapour fireplace, and a zero-proof bar pouring adaptogenic drinks built around Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, and vitamins instead of alcohol. Phones go in lockers. Be fully present. You leave grounded, energized, and clear-headed — not depleted.
