events.event_title: Change Leadership Conference 2026: The Art and Science of Leading Change From Theory to Practice
events.event_description:
As organizations navigate AI acceleration, digital transformation, and continuous disruption, one question remains: How do we lead change and drive adoption in a way that actually works?

The Change Leadership Conference 2026 brings together leaders, transformation professionals, HR, communications, and technology practitioners to explore how multidisciplinary practices intersect to drive successful change.

Marking its 10th anniversary, this year’s conference focuses on bridging research, frameworks, and lived experience to help leaders move from theory to practice while reimagining the future of change leadership.

Topics include:
	•	The role of AI and emerging technology in leading change
	•	Building resilient, future-ready leadership capability
	•	Cross-functional collaboration between HR, Communications, Change, and Project teams
	•	Practical application of proven change frameworks
	•	Developing credibility and influence as a change leader

Attendees will experience:
	•	Practitioner-led sessions and real-world case studies
	•	Facilitated roundtable discussions
	•	Peer-level networking with leaders responsible for transformation outcomes
	•	Actionable insights they can apply immediately

This event is designed for professionals accountable for digital transformation, organizational change, and innovation initiatives who want practical tools, strategic perspective, and meaningful connections.

Location: Toronto and Virtual

Join us as we explore what it truly takes to lead change in a rapidly evolving, tech-driven world.

events.event_title: Loop Tech Talk 2: The Proxy Pattern: How Next.js Let Us Migrate Without a Big Bang
events.event_description:
Join us for our Toronto Tech Week x Loop Tech series! 

What we'll cover: How Loop uses Vercel's Next.js as an intelligent proxy layer to incrementally migrate from a legacy Netlify app — zero downtime, one route at a time. A practical playbook for teams stuck with legacy front ends who want to modernize without betting the business.

Key topics:
Next.js rewrites and middleware as a migration strategy
Intelligent routing: "route exists?" → Next.js, else → Netlify legacy
Handling authentication state (Cognito cookies) across two apps
Real gotchas: the non-ASCII cookie bug that broke production
GraphQL federation + Apollo Client as the unified data layer

Come join us for a fun morning of learning, networking and starting the day strong.

events.event_title: Women in STEM Leadership Accelerator Labs
events.event_description:
Women in STEM Leadership Accelerator Labs
Connect, Accelerate & Thrive

Toronto Tech Week brings together innovators shaping the future—and women leaders in STEM are essential to that future.

Yet many accomplished women in technology and STEM fields still face challenges being heard, recognized, and advanced in leadership spaces that remain largely male-dominated. Technical expertise alone isn’t always enough. Understanding your unique leadership voice, building visibility, and navigating complex workplace dynamics are critical for advancing your career and influence.

Join Executive Coach Amy Au (Innovate You) and Hilda Gan (People Bright Consulting) for the Women in STEM Leadership Accelerator Labs, an interactive learning experience designed specifically for women leaders, senior professionals, and rising talent in STEM.

This dynamic program offers two half-day accelerator sessions, each focused on practical leadership development and real-world application.

In the Morning Accelerator Lab, participants will be introduced to the Fascinate® System, a research-based framework that helps individuals identify their unique communication style. Through guided exercises and real-world scenarios, participants will learn how to communicate with greater confidence, clarity, and influence.

The Afternoon Accelerator Lab focuses on career advancement and leadership visibility with their Fascinate Advantages in mind. Through interactive discussions and practical strategies, participants will explore how to navigate career pathways, amplify their voices in leadership settings, build strong relationships with demanding stakeholders, and overcome challenges often faced in male-dominated industries.

Participants may attend one or both sessions depending on their interests and schedule.

Between sessions, attendees are invited to join a complimentary guided tour (12:00–1:00 PM) of the inspiring “The 52: Women Who Shaped Toronto” exhibition in the building.

Come ready to connect with other trailblazing women, accelerate your leadership growth, and thrive in your career.

events.event_title: Learn to Work with an AI Agent — Claude Cowork Workshop
events.event_description:
For 40 years, using a computer meant learning its language—menus, buttons, shortcuts. Claude Cowork inverts that. The computer learns your language, reads your files, connects to your tools, and acts on your behalf. A full-day, hands-on workshop for knowledge workers ready to work alongside AI.

Who this is for
You're a knowledge worker, with a technical or builder mindset. You work in operations, finance, marketing, sales, product, HR, or a similar role. Maybe you've used Claude before, maybe you haven't. What matters is that you're comfortable with technology and ready to learn something new.

You should be the kind of person who can install an app, manage files on your computer, and follow instructions without hand-holding. We'll teach you everything else.

You should apply if:

You want to learn how to work alongside an AI agent, not just chat with one
You have real workflows you'd like to automate or improve
You're evaluating Cowork for yourself or your team and want hands-on experience
You have (or are willing to get) a Claude Pro, Teams, Max, or Enterprise subscription
Interested in Claude Code for developers or more technical courses? Antfluent offers a dedicated Claude Code workshop for technical teams. Reach out at info@antfluent.com for details.

What you'll learn
Fundamentals of working with Claude: How Claude actually works and the practical knowledge that makes you a better user. You'll understand tokens, context, and system prompts so you can manage cost. You'll learn prompt engineering so you can get better results. And you'll learn the cost curve: when to pay for expensive intelligence and when to combine your own judgment with effective techniques and less expensive models.

Working from Chat to Agents: What changes when Claude goes from answering questions to doing work. The Cowork mental model: an AI agent running on your machine with access to your files, your calendar, your email.

Building blocks to automation: The. building blocks of Cowork: connectors, skills, plugins, and scheduled tasks. Two guided hands-on sessions where you connect your real tools, build a custom workflow, and set it up to run automatically. By the end of the afternoon, you'll have something running on your machine that wasn't there this morning.

Requirements before the workshop
Bring:

Your laptop (macOS or Windows) with Claude Desktop installed.
An active Claude subscription (Pro, Teams, Max, or Enterprise) with Cowork access.
We provide:

Full-day instructor-led workshop
Catered lunch and coffee and snacks
Printed quick-reference materials
Post-workshop resources for continued learning.
Pricing
Standard: $449 CAD

Testimonial Rate: $349 CAD — $100 off for helping us tell the story — a short on-camera testimonial and permission to share it as part of Antfluent's training portfolio.

About
Gabriel Velazquez Lopez — Founder & Principal, Antfluent

Gabriel has spent 20 years launching technology that matters: Canada's first 4G network at TELUS, a revamped channel partnership at Apple, the first core banking migration for a credit union in North America on AWS, then Claude on AWS.

As Anthropic's first Applied AI & Partnership lead at AWS, Gabriel built the global GTM strategy behind Claude's growth into enterprise. He launched every Claude product that reached enterprise customers: Claude for Enterprise, Education, Financial Services, Life Sciences, and Claude Code and designed AWS' Claude Code production guidance adopted by over 1,200 companies, now the third most deployed solution in the AWS Solutions Library.

Gabriel is an Anthropic Claude Certified Architect and AWS Certified Solutions Architect Professional.

Antfluent
Antfluent is Canada's first Anthropic-specialized consulting, training, and advisory boutique.

events.event_title: The Tech Expert to Leader Playbook
events.event_description:
Most engineers get promoted because they’re great at solving technical problems, only to find that leading a team requires a different set of tools. We are hosting this 4-hour workshop in Oakville to help you navigate that shift without losing your momentum or your sanity. We avoid complicated theories in favor of practical tools that help you move from being involved in every small detail to helping your team operate independently.

Who this is for:
* This workshop is for technical experts or engineers who have recently moved into a lead role.
* It is designed for senior team members being groomed for promotion who want to prepare for the shift in responsibilities.
* It is a fit for those who have been managing a team for a while and are looking for more structure in their daily leadership.
* We welcome leads who want to move away from the small details and focus on helping their team operate independently.

What We’ll Cover:
* We will look at how to step back from individual execution to focus on team-level oversight.
* You will learn practical ways to manage your personal bandwidth and protect your mental energy.
* We will bring simple frameworks to help you navigate the transition into management.
* We will use exercises to help you practice moving from an effective contributor to a transformational leader.

What’s Included:
* A printed leadership workbook with frameworks you can apply immediately.
* A catered networking lunch with other West-End tech leaders.
* Tech Week Special: A shorter, high-impact format designed to fit into your busy schedule.

About the Instructor:
Karen Wonders is an engineer and Certified Executive Coach with over two decades of experience in technical leadership. Holding an Executive MBA, she specializes in the pivotal transition from effective contributor to transformational leader. Karen combines deep practical experience with coaching expertise to empower technical professionals to lead with confidence and impact.

events.event_title: Unscripted: How Bias Shapes Investor Decisions — and How We Change It
events.event_description:
​ Women and Gender-Diverse founders are asked different questions — and it costs them millions in lost investment.
Join us for an unfiltered and deeply needed conversation during Toronto Tech Week as we bring together investors, founders, and ecosystem leaders to examine one of the most persistent barriers in entrepreneurship: **bias in investment decision‑making**.  

Despite the innovation, resilience, and economic impact of women and gender‑diverse entrepreneurs, funding gaps remain staggering. This panel goes beyond the statistics to explore the *real* dynamics at play — the assumptions, narratives, and unconscious biases that shape who gets funded, who gets overlooked, and what we can collectively do to change the system.

**Unscripted** means exactly that: an honest, unsanitized dialogue about the structural and cultural forces that influence investor behaviour. Our panelists will share their lived experiences navigating the investment landscape, from pitching to gatekeepers to challenging outdated norms about what a “successful entrepreneur” looks like. Investors will also speak candidly about the pressures, blind spots, and decision‑making patterns that shape their choices — and how the industry can evolve toward more equitable, evidence‑based practices.

Together, we’ll explore:  
- How bias shows up in pitch rooms 
- The myths and stereotypes that continue to shape investor perceptions  
- What founders can do — and shouldn’t have to do — to navigate biased systems  
- How investors can adopt more inclusive frameworks and accountability measures  
-What collective action across the ecosystem could look like

Designed for founders, investors, ecosystem partners, and anyone committed to building a more equitable and inclusive entrepreneurial economy, the conversation will be bold, practical, and grounded in real‑world experience, with space for meaningful connection, reflection, and action.

​This event is part of Canadian Women's Chamber of Commerce’s Flip the Script Investment Readiness Program a self‑paced online learning experience that helps women‑identified and non‑binary entrepreneurs master a communication style proven to secure up to 60% more funding from investors through a licensed CanWCC AI‑powered training simulation.

​For more visit : www.canwcc.ca /https://fts.canwcc.ca/

Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 26–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com

events.event_title: Media Training 101 for Tech Founders: Tell Stories That Actually Land
events.event_description:
This 90-minute, high-impact session is a crash course in how to show up in media interviews with clarity and control. Designed for growth-stage tech founders, you’ll learn how PR can help build trust and credibility with both customers and investors, what reporters actually look for, how to avoid common interview mistakes, and how to turn your story into something worth covering.

You’ll learn how to:
Land your key messages in under 20 seconds
Bridge tough or off-topic questions

The noise around AI and PropTech has never been louder. The question for 2026 isn’t whether technology is transforming real estate, it’s which signals are worth following, and which structures are being built to last. Join our one-day summit for the people building, funding, and operating the future of real assets. Our summit brings together limited partners, real estate developers, energy innovators, and portfolio company leaders for a full day of candid conversation, live product demonstrations, and collaborative problem-solving; practitioners-deploying capital, building products, operating assets-working through the questions that actually matter in 2026.

events.event_title: The Business Case for Safe Autonomy: Efficiency, Accountability, and Remote Oversight
events.event_description:
Autonomy can deliver real efficiency , lower operating costs, better utilization, and more consistent service , but only when deployments are designed with clear accountability and a credible safety approach. 

This virtual session is for founders and companies evaluating autonomy as an operational advantage. We will share a practical, non-proprietary framework to assess autonomy initiatives: where ROI is most achievable, how to define measurable efficiency outcomes, what “safe enough” means in day-to-day operations, and how remote oversight (human-in-the-loop support and escalation) helps maintain control, uptime, and responsible decision-making. 

We will also cover common reasons pilots fail, mis-scoped use cases, unclear success metrics, missing incident processes, and stakeholder misalignment and how to structure pilots that can progress toward scalable deployments. Attendees will leave with a buyer-ready checklist for evaluating autonomy solutions and designing pilots that satisfy both efficiency goals and safety requirements. Live Q&A included.

events.event_title: Shadow AI vs. The Secure Stack: Grounding the 98% Reality of Unsanctioned AI
events.event_description:
According to recent studies, 98% of of orgs have employees using AI without permission, with 38% of employees sharing sensitive data unwittingly. Join Kind Logic AI for a grounded, hype-free breakdown of "Shadow AI." We’ll move past the alarmism to show how leadership can bridge the gap between unsanctioned usage and a secure, governed tech stack.

----

While leadership teams debate AI policy, their employees have already moved ahead. Recent data shows that 98% of organizations face "Shadow AI"—unauthorized usage of public LLMs and unvetted tools that boost individual productivity but create massive governance, security, and data leakage risks.

In this executive working session, Kind Logic’s co-facilitators Jordan Loewen-Colón, PhD (Queen's University) and Alonso Gutierrez (Systems Engineer, ex-Twitter/Huawei) strip away the theatrical jargon to address the reality of the modern workforce.

What we will cover:

The Signal in the Shadow: Why your team is bypassing IT and what those "Shadow" tools tell you about your organization’s actual needs.

The Systems Reality: A technical look at how personal accounts and unvetted APIs interact with proprietary data, and why "blocking" is rarely the solution.

Building the Secure Stack: Practical frameworks for transitioning from "Ghost" usage to an institutional AI infrastructure that is both empowering and governed.

Beyond the Vibe Check: How to replace vague AI principles with rigorous, programmatic assessments that hold up in a real institution.

This is a practical session for leaders who need to manage the AI that is already inside their building today.

Hosted by KindLogic AI Labs — Bringing practical signal to executive decision-making.

events.event_title: The Founder’s Canvas: A Creative Strategy Workshop for Technical Founders Stuck on a Startup Problem
events.event_description:
About the Event

Most founders don’t fail because of a technical problem. They fail because they get stuck in a logic loop, trying to solve new challenges with the same analytical thinking that created them.
When that happens, the real constraint isn’t technology or resources. It’s perspective.
If you’re a technical founder who tends to double down on analysis when a problem gets hard, this workshop will help you unlock new solution paths without abandoning structured thinking.

During the workshop, we’ll run a live startup strategy experiment.
Using real challenges from the startup world, we’ll map the problems and push them through structured creative frameworks to uncover solution paths most founders would never consider.
By the end of the session, you’ll walk away with a new way of reframing strategic problems and a set of creative thinking tools you can use whenever your startup hits a wall.

Inside the Founder’s Strategy Lab, we will:

• Break down hidden assumptions behind the challenge
• Reframe the problem using the Founder’s Canvas
• Generate unconventional solution paths
• Pressure-test ideas using systems thinking

This workshop is intentionally small and interactive, so spots are limited.

Meet Your Facilitator

Kerry McKenzie is a program facilitator, business coach, and IT analyst who specializes in helping founders navigate the messy middle of entrepreneurship.
They’ve coached 100+ entrepreneurs and facilitated startup programs, working with founders to unlock new strategic perspectives when progress stalls.

Light fare will be provided for workshop participants. Please provide any dietary restrictions upon registration.

events.event_title: Bits & Bikes: Scaling Infrastructure in the Cloud and on the Streets
events.event_description:
Infrastructure is more than just YAML files and Kubernetes clusters. It’s also thousands of physical stations and bike locks scattered across a city. While one world lives in the cloud and the other on the pavement, both require a strong focus on reliability.

Join Josh Paulin (Sr. Engineer, Observability Platform), Gabe Velasquez (Sr. Observability Architect, Grafana Labs), and Rob Ewaschuk (Principal Engineer, Lyft Urban Solutions) for a deep dive into the parallels and pitfalls of managing systems at scale.What we’ll cover:

The Digital: Josh and Gabe will cover the evolution of a massive-scale metrics platform and the migration strategies used to modernize the stack. This talk focuses on how the Lyft team ensured the systems powering operations remained resilient while swapping the engine mid-flight.

The Physical: Rob will explore how we apply reliability engineering practices to "real-world" infrastructure like Bike Share Toronto. We’ll dive into the complexities of managing a fleet of heterogeneous, low-power IoT devices over cellular modems and the creative ways we’ve used engineering to reduce hardware theft and loss.

events.event_title: Gibberish - Mini Hackathon for the music interaction experience
events.event_description:
Music links are instant, but forgettable. You click, land on Spotify, and move on. What if there was a moment before the music that made you pause?

Gibberish is a mini hackathon exploring how interaction, design, and timing can reshape how we experience music before it reaches streaming platforms.

You’ll build on a simple demo and create your own feature. No pressure, no experience needed. Just quick ideas, fast prototypes, and seeing what actually makes someone engage.

events.event_title: How Great Teams Win in the Age of AI
events.event_description:
AI is accelerating but but team performance is not.  

As organizations adopt new tools and technologies, many are running into the same challenges: unclear ownership, slower decision-making, and growing disconnects between strategy and execution.  

This session brings together experienced leaders to unpack what it actually takes for teams to perform in the age of AI.  This is not a passive panel, it is a guided, interactive conversation.  

In this session, we will explore:  Why teams still matter in an AI-driven world The core attributes of high-performing teams The most common breakdowns teams experience today How leaders can improve decision-making, alignment, and trust.  The discussion will go beyond surface-level insights. 

Panelists will challenge assumptions, share real experiences, and work through practical scenarios that leaders are facing today.  

Attendees will leave with clearer thinking, sharper questions, and a better understanding of how to strengthen their teams in an AI-driven environment.  Part of the “How Great Teams Win in the Age of AI” Roadshow

This session is one stop in a multi-city experience bringing together leaders across North America to rethink how teams perform in an AI-driven world.

Join us in a format that works best for you:

Boston (Virtual): May 28 at 2:00 PM
New York City (In-Person): June 3
SF: TBA 
LA: TBA

Each session offers a slightly different experience, but all are grounded in the same core idea:  The next competitive advantage isn’t AI alone,  it’s how your team performs with it.

We’d love to see you at one (or more) of the stops.

Learn to negotiate your salary, promotion, or next offer with confidence! This hands-on workshop for Women in Tech covers live scripts, practice rounds, and proven strategies. This workshop will equip you to ask for more and get it confidently! Includes print materials & goodie bag. 

Learn to negotiate your salary, promotion, or next offer with confidence! This hands-on workshop for women in tech covers real scripts, live practice rounds, and proven strategies. Women in tech are still earning less and negotiating less. This workshop will teach you how to ask for more and get it confidently! Includes print materials and a swag bag.

🌟Strategic Salary & Promotion Negotiation Workshop for Women in Tech🌟This hands-on workshop will teach you how to ask for more and get it confidently!

You work in tech. You move fast. You ship real impact. But when it’s time to talk about salary, promotion, or offers? Suddenly it feels… unclear.
This high-energy, hands-on workshop is built for women in tech, especially early-career professionals, who are ready to:
- Negotiate a new offer

- Transition into higher-impact roles

In tech, your value compounds fast. Your compensation should too!
If you’ve ever thought,“I know I’m good at what I do… I just don’t know how to say it in a salary conversation.” This room is for you. 
Negotiation isn’t about being aggressive. It’s about being prepared, strategic, and clear.

The 10-Minute Raise Strategy: How to structure a clear, compelling raise conversation.

events.event_title: The Last Skill: Self-Directed Learning in the Age of AI
events.event_description:
Most people use AI to skip the learning. The best builders use it to accelerate it.

The difference isn't the tool. It's the system underneath.

In a world where AI raises the floor for everyone, the only real edge left is how fast and how deeply you can build genuine expertise. That requires more than prompting — it requires knowing how to learn.

The Last Skill is a 90-minute workshop for founders, operators, and builders who want to use AI the way expert learners do.

→ Metacognition — use AI to think against your own understanding, not just confirm it
→ Encoding — use AI to build deep expertise, not the illusion of it
→ Deliberate Practice — use AI to design practice that actually transfers to real problems

You won't leave with slides. You'll leave with a system.

Hosted by Diego Vera, founder of Self Learners — Toronto-based learning science practitioner and Business Insider-featured educator who has worked with 100+ students and teams.

events.event_title: The Great Conversation: How Technologists Shape History
events.event_description: Within each of us there is a drive to move the human project forward. We are told to maximize our agency, to "just do things."  But how do we know which things are worth doing?On May 27th, The Toronto Society, in collaboration with Notion, Cursor, and Ambition, presents The Great Conversation, a lecture on the history of technology.We have been telling the story of technology backwards. Our tools—steam engines, silicon chips—take center stage, but these artifacts are the lagging indicators of progress, not the drivers. Tools are the image of their creators. If we are to understand technology, we must begin by understanding technologists.To be a technologist is to have a theory of what it means to be human, and to make that theory concrete through the craft of engineering and design.For Toronto Tech Week, we are going to profile extraordinary individuals in the history of technology—many Canadian—and trace their lineage to our present day. We will show you what it looks like to understand yourself not merely as a “builder”, but as a historic actor with a stake in politics and culture. Technology is a conversation that stretches across millennia. You have been part of this conversation your entire life. You just haven't been formally introduced.Before and after the lecture our lead sponsors, joined by Ambrook, Positive Sum, Colossus and many other publications will join us in the lobby to share reading materials and discuss the ways they are shaping this conversation.

events.event_title: From Research to Clinic: Deploying Voice AI in Healthcare (Reliability, Compliance, ROI)

A practical session on deploying voice AI in healthcare: reliability, safety boundaries, privacy/compliance, and measurable ROI. Includes a hands-on workflow teardown and a roundtable with builders and operators. Hosted by TalkToMedi with entrepreneurship partners.

Full Description: Phone calls still run healthcare, but clinics are overwhelmed, patients wait on hold, and front-desk teams burn out. This event brings together entrepreneurs, builders, and healthcare operators to explore how voice AI can agentically and measurably improve patient access.What you’ll experience- Reliability patterns for voice agents (handoff, edge cases, fail-safes)- Privacy/compliance realities (high-level, non-legal)- Measuring ROI (call minutes saved, booking success, deflection rates)- Live demos of healthcare voice AI handling real call flows: booking/rescheduling, clinic FAQs, structured intake, and safe escalation to staff.- Student entrepreneurship spotlight with NEXT Canada–style “build in public” learnings and practical advice for shipping in regulated environments.Who should attend- Clinic leaders, healthcare operators, and innovators exploring AI in access + operations- Founders building applied AI products- Investors and ecosystem partners supporting healthcare innovationAgenda 6:30 Networking + light refreshments7:00 Short talks (5–7 min) Live demos + audience Q&A7:45 Roundtable + matchmaking8:30 WrapWe’ll prioritize attendees working on healthcare operations or applied AI. Waitlist will be enabled.

events.event_title: Float + Reset Sunrise Paddleboard
events.event_description:
​Part of the Kolbe 15 Rain or Shine series.
​The calm before the tech storm. Start your morning on the water as the sun rises, take a moment to be present, set the mood for the day.

​We meet at Cherry Beach at 6am, get on the water, and paddle out as the sun comes up. No agenda, no pitch, no performance. Just you, the water, and the people around you.

​Once we're out, we stop. For 20 minutes we close our eyes, put the week away, and just be present with the sound of the water, the air, the moment. No experience required. No instruction. Just space to breathe.

​Then we have tea and coffee on the water, a small snack, and talk. The kind of conversation that happens when you're floating on a lake at sunrise with people who get it.

​We paddle back by 8am. You're at your desk by 9am. But you'll feel different for the rest of the day.

events.event_title: Watts of Innovation: Toronto Tech Week Ride
events.event_description:
Most Tech Week events start with coffee. This one starts at 25 km/h.

Watts of Innovation is a 20–25km road ride for experienced cyclists in tech. This is not a casual ride. Expect a steady pace, tight group riding, and a focused route.

We start early so you can ride hard and still make it back for Tech Week events. 

The route heads west along the waterfront, into High Park for ~1 hour of laps and optional climbs, then finishes with coffee.

This is intentionally a niche ride for people who take both building and riding seriously.

Hosted by Unified.to, Forum Ventures, and Mobile Capital Network.

Schedule
6:30 AM — Depart Princess Gates (primary start)
~6:45 AM — Pickup at Colborne Lodge & Lakeshore
6:45–7:45 AM — High Park laps + optional climbs
~8:15–8:30 AM — Coffee at Cherry Bomb

Ride Details
Distance: ~20–25 km
Pace: 25–27 km/h average
Format: Group ride + High Park laps
This is not a beginner or casual ride.

Route
Martin Goodman Trail (westbound)
High Park loops (optional Ellis climb)
Exit via High Park Blvd → Roncesvalles
Finish at Cherry Bomb

Safety
Helmets required
Call out hazards (holes, bumps, debris)
Obey all traffic signals and stop signs
Hold your line and ride predictably

Requirements
Road bike required
No city bikes or rentals
Comfortable riding in a group at pace

trove is Toronto's urban sanctuary for holistic health offering numerous restorative modalities, guided down-regulating classes, IV therapy, and practitioner treatments all under one roof, and curated to your needs. trove offers an elevated wellness experience that feels like a retreat from the rush of the city—convenient, luxurious, and deeply restorative.

events.event_title: ALL IN Talks Toronto
events.event_description:
Building on the success of ALL IN, Canada’s largest artificial intelligence and technology event, ALL IN Talks Toronto will bring together Ontario’s leaders, builders, and decision-makers shaping our technological future. Designed as a focused, human-scale gathering, the event connects the province’s ecosystem around a shared ambition: turning advanced technologies into real-world impact.

Ontario stands out as one of the most advanced AI and technology ecosystems in North America, anchored by the Toronto-Waterloo corridor. With globally recognized research institutions, a dense concentration of startups and scale-ups, and a strong culture of industry collaboration, the province offers a unique environment where innovation moves efficiently from labs to market. ALL IN Talks Toronto is where Ontario-based solutions are showcased as technologies ready to be adopted, deployed, and scaled across organizations and industries.

At a time of rapid technological acceleration and increasing competitive pressure, ALL IN Talks Toronto speaks directly to adopters. The event provides private- and public-sector leaders with a credible, collaboration-driven space to explore proven AI and technology solutions, examine concrete use cases across key sectors, and engage directly with the teams building trustworthy, responsible, and commercially viable innovations, aligned with today’s operational, regulatory, and governance realities.

Extending the momentum of ALL IN to Ontario, ALL IN Talks Toronto creates a platform for collaboration, partnerships, and action. It positions Ontario—and Toronto in particular—as a central driver of Canada’s AI and technology leadership, and as a place where ambition is translated into execution, adoption, and measurable impact.

events.event_title: Privacy and Pilates: Getting Privacy Fit for Tech Leaders (8am)
events.event_description:
You don't need a legal team or a compliance department to get privacy right - you need to know what actually matters for a company at your stage.

This is a small, informal session designed for Toronto tech leaders who want a clear-eyed picture of their privacy obligations without the jargon. In 30 focused minutes, you'll walk away knowing what you're required to have, what you're probably missing, and where to focus first.

Before (or after) the talk (we'll split into groups), we'll move through a guided pilates session to match - because if you're going to strengthen your core, you might as well strengthen both of them!

Spots are limited. Comfortable clothes encouraged. Note that indemnity forms must be completed before attending the Pilates portion.

events.event_title: Two Steps Forward: Life After The 9-5
events.event_description:
Thinking of leaving your 9–5 for consulting? Join our picnic for mid-career women to connect, hear real stories from those who’ve successfully transitioned, and get practical tips on preparing financially, building clients, and navigating the move to independence.

---

🧺 This outdoor picnic is for Mid-Career Women in Business and Technology who are planning the move from a full-time role to independent consulting.

​Moving from a stable 9–5 to consulting is exciting but challenging. Many successful consultants spend 1 to 2 years preparing: building relationships, financial stability and a clear plan.

​We’ll be joined by women who have successfully made this transition. They’ll share their journeys, surprises, lessons learned, and practical advice for those preparing to take the leap.


​THIS EVENT IF FOR YOU IF YOU ARE

​👉 A mid-career woman in business and technology ready to become an independent consultant
​👉 Currently working full-time and planning your transition
​👉 Looking to learn from others and connect with like-minded peers

​
TOPICS INCLUDE
* ​Preparing strategically without jeopardizing your current job
* Developing your service offering
* Building relationships and potential clients in advance
​* educing financial risk and build foundations before leaving
​* Navigating the emotional shift from employee to independent professional

​
​EVENT VIBE
​Casual, supportive and practical. Learn from those who have taken their next steps forward.

​
​SCHEDULE

11:00 am - 11:15 am
Set up your blanket and introduce yourself

​11:15 am - 11:30 am
Consultants tell their stories

​11:30 am - 1:00 pm
Guided Q&A

​Bonus
Stay and enjoy the park and keep the conversations going after the event.

​
​LOCATION
​High Park - 🍁the maple leaf hill
From the main park entrance on High Park Ave and Bloor St West, walk along the Colborne Lodge Rd toward the Grenadier Cafe. Keep walking South until you see a geometric fountain/pool and a wooden hanging garden. Walk through the hanging garden and then down the hill. Our group will be there, on a hillside.

​
​WHAT TO BRING
​🟦 Waterproof picnic blanket
🌞 Sunscreen
🥪 Snacks and drinks
❓ Curiosity and questions

​
​RAIN PLAN

events.event_title: Bridge the Gap: building smarter legal tools
events.event_description:
Bridge the Gap: building smarter legal tools, is a one day, competitive legal tech hackathon where developers, lawyers and law students can team up to build tools that transform the lawyer and client experience. Smarter intake, real-time case visibility, and AI that makes exceptional legal service delivery the norm, not the exception.

A featured panel brings together Sam Presvelos, founder of Lexiden, a legal AI platform revolutionizing how legal services are delivered to business owners, alongside Omar Soliman, Partner at Cassels Brock & Blackwell LLP, one of Canada's top law firms, and the founder of Clearview Counsel. Together they'll share first-hand experience using AI tools to enhance their practices and deliver further value to clients.

The top two teams will each take home a $2,000 cash prize. Organic energy drinks and matcha will be provided by Drink Juice Co., with a pizza dinner and social mixer to close out the evening.

Our event is proudly co-hosted by Toronto Tech Week:
e: contact@torontotechweek.com

events.event_title: Lunch & Learned: Building an Investor Mindset
events.event_description: Lunch & Learn: Building an Investor Mindset is a structured, small-group series designed for experienced professionals evaluating exposure to private markets and early-stage opportunities. It is particularly relevant for advisors to family-owned enterprises exploring patient capital strategies, as well as operators and executives seeking a disciplined framework before deploying capital.​This is not a pitch event, demo day, or fundraising session. The in-person working lunch in Toronto establishes a practical capital allocation framework — how the venture model works in practice, how investors assess asymmetric risk and opportunity, and how capital is deployed at the pre-seed and seed stages. The session is intentionally discussion-driven and limited in size to allow for meaningful exchange, not presentations.​Participants then continue with two live online sessions that deepen the framework, covering venture exits and return mechanics, portfolio construction and follow-on strategy, and early-stage valuation logic. Together, the three sessions form a coherent learning path — moving from understanding venture mechanics to evaluating outcomes and managing capital exposure over time.​This series is designed for professionals who want more than surface-level commentary. It offers a structured, practical lens for approaching early-stage investing within a broader long-term capital strategy.

Attendees will leave with practical insights into how modern growth strategies combine data, technology, and creative storytelling to win in an AI-driven market.

events.event_title: Q2 2026 State of AI + AE (Autonomous Economy) & Investment Opportunities
events.event_description: Untapped Ventures: We're at an interesting moment in AI. The hype cycle has matured. The infrastructure bets are playing out. Agentic systems are moving from demos to production. And a new layer - what we call the Autonomous Economy - is starting to take shape in ways that matter for how companies get built and how capital gets deployed.Most of the coverage still hasn't caught up. So George is going to share his read on it directly.What we'll cover:→ The Autonomous Economy - a working definition. Not a buzzword. A framework for understanding what's actually shifting and why it matters in 2026.→ State of AI in Q2 2026: signal vs. noise. Where adoption is real, where infrastructure is consolidating, and where the application layer is opening up.→ Investment themes Untapped is watching. The 3-5 verticals and theses we're most excited about right now - with real context, not generic categories.→ What this means for founders and operators. Practical implications: what to build, what to avoid, and how to position for what's coming.→ Live Q&A. Bring your questions. George will answer them directly.Who should come:Founders building AI-native or AE-adjacent companies. Investors and allocators looking for a fund-level read on where the market is going. Operators and leaders navigating AI inside their organizations. Anyone who's tired of surface-level takes and wants real signal.

events.event_title: AI Fundamentals for Founders Workshop
events.event_description:
AI is moving fast.

New tools are dropping every week. Everyone has an opinion on what you should be using. And somehow, despite all the hype, most founders are still buried in manual work, wondering where the time savings actually are. If that sounds familiar, this workshop is for you. In one room, in one session, we're going to cut through the noise together.

What we'll cover:
The latest AI innovations worth your attention and the ones you can safely ignore
The essential tools to consider for your stack, with honest takes on what works and what doesn't
What Trevor has tested across his own ventures and the real results behind it
Frameworks, mindsets, and practical skills you can apply the same week
A live build session working through real examples together, so you leave with something tangible, not just notes

You'll come away with:
A clearer picture of where AI actually fits in your business right now
A starter toolkit matched to your stage and goals
Practical skills to run and grow your business with AI without the expensive trial and error
More confidence, less overwhelmed

Trevor will tailor the session based on who's in the room.

Before the event, you'll share a bit about your business and what you're working on. That way, the tools, examples, and workflows covered are relevant to where you actually are, not some hypothetical founder.

The goal isn't to add more subscriptions to your stack.
It's to leave better equipped to run and grow your business with AI. Freeing your time. Empowering you to move faster. Without running up an expensive testing bill figuring it out alone.
Hosted in partnership with George Brown Polytechnic, startGBC entrepreneur support centre and Toronto Tech Week.
–
Trevor Davis is a Toronto-based founder and AI marketing systems builder who runs multiple ventures almost entirely on AI-powered workflows. He'll walk you through what's actually working right now, what he's tested, and what results he's seen, so you can stop experimenting blindly and start building with intention.

events.event_title: How To Close Enterprise Deals in Founder-Led Sales
events.event_description:
As startup founders trying to sell into the enterprise, we’ve all heard these remarks before: “Great demo” then crickets, or “this is awesome but we have some other priorities right now, can we regroup in a couple of months?” We’ve also seen pilots or proof-of-concepts (POCs) that don’t lead to closed deals, difficulty getting to the right people involved, or seemingly positive conversations that don’t move the deal forward.

​The good news:  many of your lost deals were your fault.

​Many of the causes of mid-funnel deals stalling or challenges with closing deals are preventable with some basic tactics for navigating complex buyer environments.  The session will provide some immediately actionable and practical tips for diagnosing problems, keeping deals moving, and maximizing value.

events.event_title: Clio Behind the Build: Ruby X AI Live Demos
events.event_description:
Toronto's best AI builders are taking the stage and we want you in the room.
Apply to Demo — submissions close May 8th at 11:59 PM EST.

Clio is partnering with Toronto Ruby to showcase the cutting edge of the Ruby AI ecosystem. This is an event for the engineers, the builders, and the innovators who are shaping what development looks like in the age of AI.

As the global leader in legal AI, trusted by over 400,000 legal professionals worldwide, Clio built the Intelligent Legal Work Platform — a system of action powered by the world's largest legal data library, designed for a profession where accuracy and real outcomes aren't optional. That standard runs through everything we build. AI shapes how our engineers write, ship, and scale code every day.

We’re opening our doors to the engineers who are holding themselves to that same high bar of craftsmanship.

The Format: Pure Technical Showcase
We’re looking for 10 of the most innovative builders to take the stage for a live technical showcase.

Show the Stack: Show us your IDE, your terminal, or your architectural diagrams.

The Q&A: After each 5-minute demo, our Engineering Leadership will step in with a question—asking the deep, technical questions about scale, latency, and trade-offs that only a peer would ask.

The Run of Show
5:00 PM - Doors Open: Registration, food/beverages, and networking.

5:45 PM - Kickoff: Opening remarks from Clio’s CTO and a welcome from Toronto Ruby.

6:00 PM - Demos: Rapid-fire technical showcases with live Q&A from Clio’s Engineering Leadership.

7:00 PM - Open Floor: Connect directly with our Engineering and Product leadership, the Toronto Ruby community, and your fellow innovators.

Want to Demo?
Applications close May 8th. If you’ve built something that pushes the boundaries of Ruby and AI, whether it’s a custom LLM bridge, a unique agentic workflow, or a breakthrough in Ruby-based data processing, we want to see it. Apply to Demo

Want to attend? Space is limited so RSVP via Luma today, to secure your spot in the room where the real building happens.

events.event_description:
Join us for our Toronto Tech Week x Loop Tech series! 

What we'll cover: How Loop built a frontend developer experience that accelerates feature delivery while maintaining quality. From a unified component library in Storybook to seamless multi-service data fetching via GraphQL federation — and how the Next.js proxy pattern enabled a smooth migration from legacy without downtime.

Key topics:

Storybook as the single source of truth for UI components
Building and rolling out a design system alongside a platform migration
Apollo Client + federated GraphQL schema for unified data access across 10+ domain services
The proxy pattern: Next.js rewrites to seamlessly route between new and legacy apps
Vercel edge functions for auth (Cognito) and performance
Measuring impact: developer velocity and customer UX improvements

So come along, grab a beer and learn something new!

No panels, no pitches, no lanyards. Wood panelled walls, a curated guest list, and a quick-match tournament threaded through the evening.This is a curated guest list. If you’re here, someone thought you should be.Dress code: Preppy casual. Like you wandered out of a Wes Anderson film and into a boardroom.Hosted by Convictional.

events.event_title: The Toronto LLM Meetup Group - Topic Pending
events.event_description: ​​​The Toronto LLM Meetup Group is founded by Suhas Pai, CTO and Co-founder of Hudson Labs, and Lily Ren, Senior PM, B2B AI at Telus. The aim is to facilitate deep dives into LLM research topics, harnessing the skills of Toronto's vibrant AI ecosystem and promoting intellectual rigor. To this end, for each session we assemble a team of top Toronto AI scientists and practitioners to conduct an in-depth study of an emerging topic that is expected to go mainstream 6 months from now. The team presents their findings along with jumping-off points for future research at a meetup session, held at Toronto's top AI companies. Eventually we aim to spawn off community open-source research projects this way.

events.event_title: ScaleUp: Connecting Talent with Toronto's Tech Startup & SME Ecosystem
events.event_description:
Join us for ScaleUp - an immersive networking event, hosted by Humber Polytechnic, designed to connect jobseekers with tech startups and small-to-medium enterprises (SMEs). This high-energy event will foster connections between newcomers, students, career changers, and Toronto’s tech ecosystem.

Why Tech startups and SMEs? These companies are growing rapidly, deeply innovative, and often far more accessible to emerging talent. They offer faster career progression, hands-on-experience, and the opportunity to make a meaningful impact from day one.

If you are exploring your next career move, this is your opportunity to tap into a hidden job market and build valuable connections within the tech sector.

Why Attend?
- Discover career opportunities within startups and SMEs
- Build meaningful connections with employers and industry professionals
- Gain insider knowledge on navigating the tech job market
- Explore pathways to upskill or transition into in-demand tech roles

Agenda

Welcome & Registration | 5:30 pm – 6:00 pm|
Kick off the evening connecting with fellow attendees before the main program begins and enjoy complimentary food and refreshments.

Scale Up Panel Discussion - Funding, Growth and Talent for Small Business & Tech | 6:00 pm – 7:00 pm|
Gain insights from industry leaders who are shaping the future of the tech ecosystem:
- The Untapped Job Market: Discover why Startups and SMEs are the hidden gems of the job market- and how to position yourself for high-impact roles.
- Funding & Investment Opportunities: Explore the funding landscape for startups and SMEs, including research support and grant opportunities available through Humber Polytechnic that can help tech business scale.
- The Humber Advantage: Hear from employers who have successfully built talent pipelines and scaled their operations through Humber programs and services.
Following the panel, dive into one of our interactive breakout sessions designed to give you a competitive edge:

The Marketplace | 7:00 pm – 8:00 pm|
Connect directly with Startups, SMEs, and Community Organizations dedicated to boosting your employability in the tech sector.

Rapid Networking | 7:00 pm – 8:00 pm|
Engage in focused, one-on-one conversations with industry mentors and get practical advice on breaking into tech

Tech Rapid Resume Reviews | 7:00 pm – 8:00 pm|
Receive personalized feedback from career coaches to strengthen your resume and stand out to employers.

Scale & Play (Board Game Lounge)| 7:00 pm – 8:00 pm|
Prefer a more relaxed setting? Unwind, connect, and network informally through interactive games in our board game lounge.

Explore What’s Next
Learn more about opportunities to advance your career through:
Humber’s Software Development Bridging Program for newcomers (15-week training program with employment support)
Humber Downtown Programs-Postgraduate certificates in AI, Machine Learning, Cybersecurity, and Governance
Humber’s upcoming Master of Computer Science - Cybersecurity Operations and Management

Spaces are limited - register now to secure your spot.

events.event_title: Women In Tech Meetup - A CodePub Workshop
events.event_description:
What is Code Pub?
​The Code Pub is an international network with events for women and gender diverse individuals who want to learn more about tech. Our goal is empowering every individual in the tech industry by creating a platform where one can be inspired and inspire others.
The Code Pub was created by Netlight in 2013 with the purpose of increasing the number of women and gender diverse individuals in the tech industry. By lowering the threshold for getting started with programming and highlighting female role models within tech, we believe that we can make a difference.

​Join us on May 28 for dinner, drinks and a friendly hands-on session: Build and Program Your Own Plant Moisture Sensor! 
​Ever wondered how to make a plant "talk" to your software? In this workshop, you’ll work with an ESP32 microcontroller to build a soil moisture sensor, dip your toes into embedded programming, and stream real-time readings into a shared dashboard.
​No technical experience necessary- just curiosity and enthusiasm! Whether you’re a beginner or a seasoned pro, you’ll leave with new skills and new friends.

events.event_title: How Cybersecurity protects and grows Revenue (without buying an Arsenal of tools)
events.event_description:
Description: How Cybersecurity protects Revenue Operations and steers revenue growth (without buying 20 tools)

Headlines
1) A practical and user-friendly lens to securing your Revenue operations
2) A short How to: Leveraging Cybersecurity as a Revenue Generator

Traditionally, cybersecurity has served as a digital guardian, protecting an organisation’s systems, data, and infrastructure from cybercriminals.

But in the age of AI, cyberattacks are multiplying faster than browser tabs during a late-night research rabbit hole. SMBs and enterprises alike are facing a constant stream of phishing attempts, credential theft, ransomware, and AI-powered social engineering.

The challenge today is not simply buying more security tools. That is infact counterproductive. The challenge is to design a cybersecurity posture that actually aligns with how your organisation operates and grows.

This session explores cybersecurity not just as a defensive function, but as a strategic lever for protecting AS WELL AS enabling revenue. (Approx Total: 90 -120 minutes)

The talk covers two key areas: (Approx 60-75 minutes)

A practical Cybersecurity Posture (for SMBs and growing teams; Enterprises also welcome) that ensures Revenue Protection:

Cybersecurity as a Revenue Enabler:

A well-designed security posture does more than prevent breaches. It improves uptime, protects brand trust, safeguards sales channels (like email and web infrastructure), and ultimately acts as a catalyst for revenue growth.

In a world overflowing with attack types and security products, how do you decide what actually matters?

Using the Pareto Principle, we’ll explore how organisations can focus on the relatively smaller but highly impactful bits of security controls that reduce real-world risks almost entirely while protecting their most important assets.

Workshop 
1) Audit your organisation’s cybersecurity posture
2) Evaluate the domain and email security health

Think of it as a quick “cyber hygiene check-up” for both organisations and individuals. Demo of tools you can use

Celebrate where you've come from and where you're going next in a room where you'll actually save a number afterward, have a laugh over drinks, and ask the "embarrassing" question because we're women lifting each other up. ​WVG is a student venture community and talent network based out of Waterloo, providing pro-bono consultancy for founders from ideation through Series A. Collectively, we’ve supported ventures in raising $8.8M+, hosted flagship programming on founder education, and curated the rooms where teams form and hires happen.

​Alex Garnett, Developer Relations, Bluesky

events.event_title: Speak Easy: Pitch Lab (Toronto Tech Week Edition)
events.event_description:
Your ideas are good. The question is: do they land?

​​Tech Week is full of conversations that matter — the ones that open doors, shape decisions, and move things forward.

​​This is your practice room.

​​Speak Easy: Pitch Lab is a hands-on evening for founders and ambitious professionals who want to sharpen how they pitch themselves, their ideas, or their next big move.

​​No lectures. No awkward spotlight. Real skill-building in a setting that doesn’t feel like a workshop.

​​How it works: Order a drink and pick up your Pitch Lab Passport, a self-guided practice card developed by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, executive communication expert and founder of Speech & Mind. As you mingle, experiment with techniques like Strategic Pausing, Vocal Authority, and Grounded Presence. Complete your Passport and walk away with sharper communication, stronger presence, and practical tools you’ll use the very next day.

​​You won’t leave with tips. You’ll leave having practiced and felt the difference.

​​WHAT TO EXPECT 
✦ Curated, growth-minded crowd 
✦ Actionable tools you’ll use immediately 
✦ A research-backed mini guide to take home 
✦ Bring your pitch to get feedback from Dr. Ozturk

​​RUN OF SHOW 
6:30–6:45 PM — Arrival & check-in 
6:45–7:45 PM — Pitch Lab 
7:45–8:30 PM — Open networking

​​🏛️ About the Host 
Speech & Mind helps leaders and ambitious professionals communicate with clarity, confidence, and presence in high-stakes moments.
Founded by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, the firm combines behavioral research with practical strategy to elevate performance when it matters most.

events.event_title: Why Founders Waste Months on the Wrong Decisions and How to Avoid It
events.event_description:
What if the biggest risk in your startup is not execution, but the assumptions you are executing on?

Many founders spend months building, selling, or scaling before they have clearly tested whether the core assumptions behind their business are true. This working session helps founders and startup operators identify those hidden assumptions and test the riskiest ones before committing too much time, money, and energy.

Through real startup examples, we will examine why some convenient products still fail to create willingness to pay, why technically strong products can break down in real-world use, and why audience growth does not always become business value.

Participants will leave with a practical decision framework for identifying key assumptions across demand, product, business model, and growth; prioritizing which assumptions to test first; and designing low-cost experiments before building, selling, or scaling too far.

This is not a motivational startup talk. It is a practical working session for early-stage founders, student founders, pre-seed and seed-stage teams, and startup operators who want to make clearer decisions and avoid wasting months in the wrong direction.

No slides. No mercy.

Natural language is the only weapon. Pure speed and logic as the barrier between idea and execution, incinerated live on stage. This is e-sports for the AI-native builder generation. One ships. The rest go home. Witness the future of building at terminal velocity. 

Costumes encouraged.

events.event_title: Hackathon: AgentShyft
events.event_description:
AgentShyft Hackathon – Build with Agentic AI.

We’re excited to announce that ShyftLabs will be hosting AgentShyft — a vibe coding hackathon on May 29, 2026, in downtown Toronto. This is your team’s chance to compete for a share of a $6,000 prize pool — and that’s not all. The strongest ideas may also unlock real funding opportunities to continue building beyond the event.

What’s the Challenge? 
Agentic AI isn’t just coming — it’s already here.

AgentShyft is your opportunity to build with it, not just talk about it. 

Teams will: 
Propose a problem and solution powered by agentic AIBuild using (ShyftLabs’ Continuum framework). 
Create everything from scratch on the day — no pre-built projects allowed. 

Team Structure: Minimum: 2 members, Maximum: 3 members. 
So… start mustering your team. 

How It Works 
1. Submit your idea by May 10, 5:00 PM EST. Include your team name, members, and a short description of your idea. 
2. Get selected. Shortlisted teams will be notified by May 17 at 1:00 PM EST and invited to participate. 
3. Build 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM: Live demo of Continuum Framework 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM: Hackathon build time
4. Present. Each team delivers a 3-minute live demo of their solution to the judges. 
5. Happy Hour Prizes 
1st Place: $3,000
2nd Place: $2,000
3rd Place: $1,000
Total Prize Pool: $6,000

Key Dates: 
Idea Submission Deadline: May 10, 5:00 PM EST. 

Selection Notification: May 17, 1:00 PM EST. 
Event Day: May 29, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM + Happy Hour, Funding Opportunities. Great ideas shouldn’t stop at the finish line. Teams with strong, viable concepts will be considered for funding support and follow-on opportunities to continue building after the event. 

What Judges Are Looking For: A product built entirely on the day. A real problem with a clear solution. A sharp, compelling 3-minute pitch. 

Submit Your Idea Deadline: May 10, 5:00 PM EST. 

Ready to participate? Complete the form and secure your spot. 

Form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScHNVJ8rQhi53dN1IU7Or4zWr2wP83Cs3AtRQSm7OSToZVkBg/viewform



Happy coding,
ShyftLabs

events.event_title: AI Meets Real Estate
events.event_description: Toronto’s housing market is a massive part of Canada’s economy, yet most of it still runs on PDFs, text messages, and outdated CRMs.At Toronto Tech Week, Daniel Foch, co-host of The Canadian Real Estate Investor Podcast and Chief Real Estate Officer at Valery.ca, will share how he is building what may be Canada’s first AI-powered real estate brokerage.This is not a generic AI talk. It is a real case study from inside the industry.Daniel will walk through how he is using AI agents to automate prospecting, follow-up, deal analysis, content creation, CRM management, and admin work that used to require full-time staff. He will break down the stack behind Realist.ca, Valery.ca, and Homie, and how media, community, and software are being combined into a vertical real estate operating system.We will also zoom out to the bigger picture. Toronto’s condo market is slowing. Construction is collapsing. Regulation is tightening. Margins are shrinking. In moments like this, industries either modernize or fall behind.If you are a founder, operator, investor, or real estate professional who wants to understand how AI is actually being deployed in a traditional, regulated industry, this session will give you a practical and honest look at what works, what breaks, and what comes next.Expect tactical insights, live workflow examples, and a candid discussion about what the future of the realtor looks like in an AI-driven world.

events.event_title: Talent, Technology and Trust: Launch of the Council of Ontario Universities’ AI Task Force Report
events.event_description:
Artificial intelligence is advancing at an unprecedented pace—reshaping industries, redefining work, transforming student learning, and accelerating global competition in real time. Jurisdictions that move quickly will lead; those that hesitate risk falling behind. Ontario is at a pivotal moment—and must act decisively.

On Friday, May 29, 2026, the Empire Club of Canada will convene a thought-provoking panel bringing together leaders from universities, industry, and Canada’s AI ecosystem to examine how Ontario can respond with urgency and turn this moment into sustained economic growth, productivity gains, and global competitiveness—while maintaining public trust.
Drawing on the insights of the Council of Ontario Universities’ AI Task Force, this discussion will focus on the actions required now to move beyond early-stage adoption toward leadership in the global AI economy. The conversation will examine how Ontario can rapidly prepare students for an AI-driven workforce through accelerated curriculum transformation and AI-enabled learning; build secure, Canadian-based data and computing infrastructure to ensure sovereignty and strategic control; and support industry in scaling AI adoption to drive innovation, investment, intellectual property creation, and productivity across key sectors.

The panel will feature Vivek Goel, President and Vice-Chancellor, University of Waterloo and Chair of the COU AI Task Force, a leading voice on advancing AI talent and responsible adoption; Shannon Bell, Executive Vice President, Chief Digital Officer and Chief Information, OpenText, with deep expertise in scaling Canadian innovation in highly competitive global markets; and Glenda Crisp, President and CEO of the Vector Institute, at the forefront of Canada’s AI research and talent ecosystem. Together, they will bring a cross-sector perspective on what it will take for Ontario to act decisively and compete at speed. The panel will be moderated by Vass Bednar, Managing Director, Canadian Shield Institute. 
This event will also serve as the official public launch of the Council of Ontario Universities’ AI Task Force report.  Attendees will gain early access to its findings and engage directly with leaders shaping Ontario’s response to one of the most consequential technological shifts in decades.

Join us for this timely discussion for policymakers, leaders, and innovators seeking to understand not just how AI will shape Ontario’s future—but what must be done now to lead.

Horizon Legacy invites the Toronto tech community — and visitors traveling in for Toronto Tech Week — to experience how robotics and automation are transforming housing delivery in Canada.

With over 70 years of experience in real estate development, Horizon Legacy is demonstrating how innovation can move beyond pilot projects and deliver real homes and thriving communities. This open house and community BBQ showcases a completed neighbourhood built using on-site construction robotics working alongside skilled trades, offering guests the chance to tour finished homes and see how construction automation is already being deployed at scale.

The event connects technology, housing innovation, and community impact, making it an ideal stop for those traveling from Ottawa, Kingston, and Montreal to attend programming during Toronto Tech Week. Located along the corridor between these cities and Toronto, the event offers a unique opportunity to see how technology is addressing one of Canada’s most urgent challenges: delivering housing faster, more safely, and at scale.

Guests can enjoy guided site tours, community programming, and remarks with local leaders celebrating the successful delivery and strong community reception of this project.

Location: 425 Fourth Street, Gananoque, ON.

Event highlights
• Guided tours of completed homes
• Community BBQ and open house atmosphere

events.event_title: AI in the Dev Trenches: A Roundtable on What's Actually Working
events.event_description:
Every dev team is experimenting with AI, but most conversations about it happen on stage, not at the table. This roundtable flips that. Developers, engineering managers, and technical leaders sit together and share what's actually working in their day-to-day: the tools, the workflows, the wins, and the failures. No keynotes. No pitches. Just practitioners learning from each other.

This is a structured peer conversation, not a panel. Each participant gets the floor to share how their team is using AI across the software development lifecycle: from code generation and review to testing, debugging, documentation, and deployment. After each share, the table opens up for questions, pushback, and "we tried that too" moments. The goal is to leave with tactics you can bring back to your team on Monday.

Topics we expect to surface include:

- Where AI pair programming tools deliver real productivity gains versus where they create false confidence
- How teams are integrating AI into code review, QA, and CI/CD pipelines
- Managing the learning curve when rolling out AI tools across a dev team
- The impact on junior developer growth when AI handles boilerplate
- Security, IP, and compliance considerations engineering leaders are navigating
- What's overhyped and what's quietly transforming how teams ship software

This roundtable is for you if you write code, manage people who write code, or make tooling decisions for a development team. Whether you're deep into AI-assisted development or just getting started, you'll find value in hearing what peers across Toronto's tech ecosystem have learned through real implementation.

Space is limited to keep the conversation intimate and high-signal. Come ready to share and learn.Hosted by HireJosie (hirejosie.ai), an AI receptionist for small businesses.

events.event_title: THE PITCH ROOM: Live. Interactive. Built for Founders
events.event_description:
You don't need a better idea. You need a better delivery.

Most founders lose the room in the first 60 seconds — not because of their deck, but because their nervous system takes over.

Under pressure, your voice and body language shift — often without you realizing it. Those signals shape how you're perceived before your content has a chance to land. If you haven't trained for that, they quietly work against you.

The Pitch Room is a fully interactive session led by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, executive communication expert and founder of Speech & Mind — grounded in neuroscience and the psychology of persuasion. 

Each round targets a specific element: how you project authority, signal confidence, and hold attention under pressure. You'll refine your delivery, identify your blind spots, and apply adjustments in real time, so your delivery becomes as strong as your ideas.

What you'll walk away with:
- A clear framework for pitches that land
- Techniques for speaking with authority and precision
- Tools to stay steady and in control under pressure
- What investors notice before your message even lands

Built for: Founders who are done leaving rooms wondering what went wrong.

About the Host 
Speech & Mind helps leaders and ambitious professionals communicate with clarity, confidence, and presence in high-stakes moments. Founded by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, the firm combines behavioral research with practical strategy to elevate performance when it matters most.

events.event_title: The Future of Sport: Where Technology and Capital Converge
events.event_description:
Toronto’s innovation ecosystem comes alive every year during Toronto Tech Week (TTW) (May 25–29, 2026) — a city-wide series of founder-, investor-, and community-led events exploring the ideas shaping the future of technology and entrepreneurship in Canada.
Last year, I hosted the only event focused on the intersection of women, sport and technology, and the response was incredible:
120 attendees (sold out), 200+ person waitlist, Fireside chat with Meghan Chayka (CEO, Stathletes - partnered with Fanduel), Supported by organizations like NBA Canada, SickKids Foundation, and STACKT market

The demand for these conversations became clear. So this year I'm expanding the conversation beyond technology to explore how capital and investment are transforming the future of sport globally.

The event will have 1) Executive panel that will convene investors, leaders from enterprise technology, and sports organizations and 2) A fireside chat, with notable athletes, that will focus on investing in community—supporting fans and networks that strengthen it—and empowering youth today to become the leaders of tomorrow.

We’ll dive into key questions such as:
How AI, cloud infrastructure, and advanced data systems are transforming athlete performance, fan engagement, media, and operations across the sports industry
The evolving role of enterprise technology and data platforms in modern sports organizations and startups
How venture capital, private equity, and institutional investment are accelerating the growth of sport-tech startups and play a role in building the next generation of sports organizations and technologies?
Where the largest opportunities for innovation and investment exist across the global sports ecosystem and new opportunities for women's sports
Why This Conversation Matters
Sport is undergoing rapid transformation — from AI-driven analytics and fan engagement to record franchise valuations and new investment vehicles entering the industry.

Yet the insights shaping this evolution sit across different communities — operators, investors, builders, and innovators — who rarely have the opportunity to come together and exchange perspectives.

This event aims to bring those voices into the same room, creating a forum for leaders across sport, technology, and capital to share ideas and help shape the next generation of sports organizations, technologies, and investment.

With all the excitement around Toronto Tech Week, Toronto Tempo (and their announcement on building a world-class performance centre) and FIFA kicking off, we've received astounding support from notable leaders and community networks.

We’re pleased to share that on May 29th we’ll be partnering with and will be joined by leaders from:

NBA, Leah MacNab, Head of International Strategy
Deloitte, Jeff Harris, Partner - Sports & Entertainment practice

We are also expecting the presence of some honourable government officials including:
Ontario Minister of Sport, Neil Lumsden, Ministry of Ontario
Associate Minister of Mental Health & Addictions, Vijay Thanigasalam, Legislative Assembly of Ontario

On the community side, we’re bringing together a diverse group of partners across investment, sport, and culture—from venture organizations to women’s sport communities and athlete foundations. It’s shaping up to be a really unique cross-section of builders, operators, and leaders who don’t often get to be in the same room.

Importantly, all of the speakers and leaders joining us have generously committed their time to be part of this conversation—this is very much about bringing the community together and creating space for shared learning and dialogue.

events.event_title: AI for the Rest of Us: A Roundtable for Small Business Owners
events.event_description:
AI is everywhere, but if you run a small business, what actually works? Join a room full of business owners for an honest conversation about the AI tools that are saving them time, the ones that flopped, and the problems nobody's solved yet.

This is not a keynote or a listening event. It's a roundtable where real business owners share what they're actually using day to day: from handling customer calls and messages, to scheduling, marketing, follow-ups, and admin that eats your week.

Whether you've tried ten AI tools or zero, bring your questions, your wins, and your horror stories. We'll cover the problems that matter most to small businesses right now:

- Keeping up with customer calls and messages when you're busy
- Scheduling and admin that takes hours out of your day
- Marketing without a marketing team
- Following up with leads before they go cold
- Figuring out what's worth paying for versus what's free and good enough

By the end, we'll compile a shared list of every tool the room recommends, sourced from the people who actually use them, not a sponsored listicle.

This event is for small business owners, solopreneurs, freelancers, and operators across any industry. No technical background needed. If you run a business and you're curious about AI, you belong in this room. Seats are limited to keep the conversation real.

events.event_title: From Agency to In-House: Creative Conversation
events.event_description:
You know that career question that lives rent-free in your head: agency, freelance, or in-house?

This talk won’t give you the answer, but it will be an honest download of what each path actually feels like from the inside. Hosted by Questrade and Friends of Figma, we’ll address the tradeoffs, the shapeshifting, the money conversations, and what it’s like to build something you're proud of inside a machine that's much bigger than you. We’ll also get into what’s changed—vibe-coding, Slack etiquette, happy hours—and what hasn’t.

After a keynote hosted by Caroline Leong (writer & strategist) and Roger Dario (designer, art director) of Questrade (formerly of Jam3/MediaMonks & Tendril), we’ll continue the conversation with both agency and in-house creatives in an open panel discussion. We’ll close out Toronto Tech Week with dinner and refreshments, and great conversation with talent from across the city. If you’re a creative, this event is for you.

events.event_title: The Rise of Agentic AI in Travel (Live Podcast Recording)
events.event_description:
​How Autonomous AI Will Transform Travel in 2026 and Beyond
​What happens when AI agents start planning, booking, and managing travel on our behalf?

​A new generation of agentic AI systems is emerging, capable of making decisions, executing complex tasks, and managing entire travel workflows. From trip planning and booking to real-time itinerary management, these autonomous systems could fundamentally reshape how travel is discovered, purchased, and experienced.

​During Toronto Tech Week, join Travel Trends Podcast and Intrepid Travel for a special live podcast recording exploring one of the most important technological shifts facing the travel industry.

​This interactive evening will feature an incredible panel of travel industry executives, startup entrepreneurs, and technology leaders discussing how agentic AI is already beginning to impact travel companies and what it means for the future of the industry in 2026 and beyond.

​Additional panelists will be announced soon.

​Unlike a traditional panel, this will be a live podcast experience, with opportunities for audience questions, interaction, and discussion during the recording.

​What to Expect

​ Live recording of the Travel Trends Podcast

​ Expert panel discussion on Agentic AI and the future of travel 

​ Audience interaction and Q&A

​ Startup demos and partner booths showcasing Toronto’s travel tech ecosystem

​ Networking party with founders, operators, investors, and travel innovators

​After the recording, stay for a fantastic networking reception designed to help connect the people shaping the future of travel. Meet fellow builders, exchange ideas, and deepen relationships across the travel and technology ecosystem.

​Whether you're a founder, travel executive, technologist, investor, or operator, this evening is an opportunity to learn, connect, and help shape how AI will transform our industry.

​Come curious. Leave connected.

​🌍 Agenda-

​5:30 PM – 6:15 PM - Registration, Networking & Live Podcast Recording

​6:15 PM – 7:00 PM - Panel Discussion: The Rise of Agentic AI and the Future of Travel

​7:15 PM – 9:00 PM - Networking & Drinks
