events.event_title: Tech Worries and Woes: Real Talk Working in the Age of AI
events.event_description:
Rounds and rounds of layoffs. Forced AI mandates. Workloads piling up on smaller and smaller teams. Are you worried about increasing surveillance and control in the workplace? Does it feel like a job in tech is no longer the “good career” that it has been in the past? And– to top it all off– you can't say anything about it at work?

You are not alone. 

Join fellow tech workers in a safe space to share your frustrations and uncertainties navigating today’s tech world. Connect with your peers, share your story, and build solidarity with fellow workers. All tech workers, whether engineering, product management, designers, analysts and/or sales are welcome.

=======
What is the Tech Workers Coalition?
​We are a coalition of workers in and around the tech industry, labor organizers, community organizers, and friends. We work in solidarity with existing movements towards social justice, workers' rights, and economic inclusion.

events.event_title: TfD Film Club: The End of the Internet
events.event_description:
Join Technologists for Democracy as we watch Dylan Reibling’s THE END OF THE INTERNET at the Revue Cinema!

From Cold War military roots to Big Tech dominance, a journey through Berlin squats, Catalan churches, and Brazilian villages reveals movements fighting to decentralize the network beyond "the cloud.”

Before each screening, audiences are invited to join a secret server hidden inside the cinema — a private, offline network, completely disconnected from the internet. Inside, a parallel version of the film unfolds: raw footage, deleted scenes, research fragments, rough notes, live chat rooms, and hidden easter eggs — materials that exist nowhere else. This ephemeral installation opens just before the screening and disappears at the end of the night.

Schedule
- ​6:00 PM - 6:30 PM - Doors open at the Revue Cinema / interactive installation
- ​6:35 PM - 8:03 PM - Film screening (88 mins)
- ​8:05 PM - 8:20 PM - Q&A
- ​8:30 PM - Drinks at Bandit Brewery (5 min walk)

events.event_title: Women Who Build: A Toronto Tech Week Fireside
events.event_description:
A curated gathering of women founders, operators, and emerging leaders for a candid fireside with accomplished women in tech —career growth, leadership, and the realities of building in this ecosystem, told without the LinkedIn filter.Then: structured networking that goes somewhere. Conversation prompts that skip the surface, easy drinks and small bites. Leave with one relationship or insight that wasn’t on your calendar this morning.Fireside Guest:

We’re joined by Upasana Sharma, a leader at TiE (one of the world’s largest entrepreneurship networks) bringing a rare perspective on what it takes to build, scale, and lead in tech across borders and industries. 

Expect an honest conversation, not a rehearsed one.Who this is for:

Women building, leading, or operating in tech: early-stage founders, product and growth operators, engineers and designers stepping into leadership, and emerging leaders looking for the room they haven’t found yet. If you’re past the introductory networking circuit and want conversations with substance, you’re in the right place.Why this one:

Toronto Tech Week is full of panels, pitches, and crowded mixers. This isn’t that. The room is small by design, the guest list is curated, and the format is built for real conversation rather than business card exchanges. You’ll hear stories that don’t get told on stage, meet women operating at your level or one step ahead, and leave with something useful —a sharper perspective, a new contact, or the answer to something you’ve been sitting with.One evening. The right room. Conversations worth having.

events.event_title: Generalist World Meetup - Toronto Tech Week
events.event_description:
On Friday May 29th, we're bringing together your fellow squiggly, spiky, dot-connecting, interdisciplinary generalists for a special Toronto Tech Week meetup!

​Join for an evening of drinks, nibbles and conversation, swapping squiggly career stories and sharing advice, experience and excitement on interesting projects, portfolio career journeys, and entrepreneurship / solopreneurship.

​This event is an official part of Toronto Tech Week :)

In all likelihood, this room will be filled with the most fascinating and multi-faceted crowd of any event in this year's Toronto Tech Week. You wouldn't want to miss it.

​Hosted by Generalist World, the breakthrough global community that's leading the charge - and writing the book - on the generalist movement.

​Sponsored by Gamma, your new favourite free-to-use AI design partner for creating effortless presentations, websites, and more. No coding or design skills required.​​ Get 1 month free of Gamma Pro with the code GENERALIST

​Organized by Dave Mence, active advocate for the generalist movement, long-time Generalist World community member, organizer of the Generalist World Toronto Community and Program Ops Manager at Notion (a decidedly generalist role).

​What is a generalist? Professionals who apply their skills across multiple domains, connect disparate ideas, or serve as "translators" between different teams and disciplines. If you've ever felt your career doesn't fit in a single box, this event is for you.

​What to expect:

​Connect with fellow generalists

​Celebrate the power of diverse skillsets and squiggly careers

​Swap squiggly career stories and practical advice

​Venue:

​This event is being hosted downtown on King St. W near Bathurst St., with easy TTC access and ample parking options. The location will be shared with each of you in your registration confirmations :)

events.event_title: Canada-Africa Innovation Festival
events.event_description:
The Canada-Africa Innovation Festival is a unique convergence of startup founders, operators, entrepreneurs, investors, local innovators, policymakers, and the enterprisingly curious.

​The Canada-Africa Innovation Festival is more than a conference, it’s an ecosystem-building initiative for startup founders, operators, key industry leaders, panels, fireside chats, workshops, pitch competitions, and collaboration opportunities with the investors and entrepreneurs shaping the future of African Innovation, from Canada to the world.

​The festival will highlight insights and success stories of African Startups and Innovators building for Africa from Canada, and from Africa to the World. Themes will cover global expansion and its implications for stakeholders, investors, and the African technology ecosystem.

​Conversations will cover detailed themes like:

​Economic Development Through Strategic Support for African Innovation

​Remittances as African Foreign Direct Investment

​Ease of doing business - Incorporating your startup in Canada

​Canada-Africa Private Equity and Venture Capital Opportunities

​Artificial Intelligence Powering Economic Growth

​African Innovation Going Global - Case Studies and the Path Forward

​Canada-Africa Trade and the AfCFTA (Africa Continental Free Trade Agreement)

​Canada-Africa Technology Investments and Relationships for increased economic growth.

​Licensing, Regulatory Compliance, and Support

​Storytelling, Marketing, and Communications for your Business.

​Digital Skills for Youth Development

​Fundraising for Startups

​Technology Talent and the Future of Work

​Technology for Business Growth.

​We can't wait to have you.
